## Thread: Who supplies ODBC API for MicroFocus COBOL 4.1 for SCO UNIX? — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### Who supplies ODBC API for MicroFocus COBOL 4.1 for SCO UNIX?

- **From:** "Stan Rydz" <rydz@erols.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-02T09:14:10-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95efgc$h2n$1@bob.news.rcn.net>`

```
I am trying to create a simple flat file on an NT system from an MF Cobol
4.1 program on a connected SCO OpenServer 5.05 UNIX system.

I was told this could be done with ODBC. I am having trouble finding ODBC
drivers for UNIX Cobol that can do this.

Any help would be appreciated.

Stan Rydz
rydz@erols.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Who supplies ODBC API for MicroFocus COBOL 4.1 for SCO UNIX?

- **From:** Stefan Meyer <meyerst@my-deja.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-02T18:34:35+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95eujq$d3o$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<95efgc$h2n$1@bob.news.rcn.net>`

```
Hi Stan,

take a look http://www.parkway-software.com
Connectware is the right product.

They have exactly what you need
- an ODBC-File-Handler
- a Server on Unix side (to increase speed ) which will serve your
requests.
- simple user access rights handling
- not expensive (IMHO)

We're using this config in our environment (Win-NT apps build with
PowerBuilder accessing ISAM-Files on Unix) and it's working :-)

But there're other vendors as well:
I've heard about RM Relativity and ???

... search this newsgroup there're a few threads about that theme (one
year or more ago).


HTH - Stefan Meyer
```

#### ↳ Re: Who supplies ODBC API for MicroFocus COBOL 4.1 for SCO UNIX?

- **From:** Vadim Maslov <vadik-nws@siber.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-02T22:56:09+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A7B3B2F.31019E41@siber.com>`
- **References:** `<95efgc$h2n$1@bob.news.rcn.net>`

```
If you need to create a flat CSV-like file
(every line is a record, it contains list of values separated by a
commma or some other character), then ODBC is not needed.

You can do the conversion directly using Data2Flat converter
from Siber Systems. More info on it at
http://siber.com/sct/datafile/data2flat.html

Regards,
Vadim Maslov
```

```
Stan Rydz wrote:
> 
> I am trying to create a simple flat file on an NT system from an MF Cobol
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> drivers for UNIX Cobol that can do this.

You would probably be better to run a Samba client on the Unix box to
mount a shared area on the NT box.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Who supplies ODBC API for MicroFocus COBOL 4.1 for SCO UNIX?

- **From:** Stefan Meyer <meyerst@my-deja.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-03T08:04:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95ge1r$k38$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<95efgc$h2n$1@bob.news.rcn.net> <3A7BCBF2.31C27E72@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
This is ok for flat non indexed files.
But if you're using indexed files you'll have problems when writing
data from both sides. The filehandler on Unix differs to the NT/DOS
filehandler which will corrupt your idx-file.

We've had exactly those problems and it was a real pain to find the
reason.

HTH - Stefan Meyer

In article <3A7BCBF2.31C27E72@Azonic.co.nz>,
```

## Thread: Doubt on Relativity performance-Record Types — 2 msgs

### Doubt on Relativity performance-Record Types

- **From:** Rech Informatica Ltda/Brazil <rovani@rech.com.br>
- **Date:** 2001-02-02T15:16:04+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95eiva$1d0$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Dear Friends,

   I am projecting a new system of revenue and I have some doubts on
how to project it to have the best possible performance with Relativity.

   Would I like to know if the primary key and the alternate keys are
used in the filters of record types?

   I have 1 file that has several record types, that are defined for
the second field of the primary key. In an alternate key this field is
defined as the first field of the alternate key. In case the keys are
used as filter of record types, I would like to know Relativity is "
intelligent " enough to use the alternate key to activate the filter of
a record type.

For instance:


           SELECT  NOBPED         ASSIGN       TO NW-DRIOBP,
                                  ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED,
                                  ACCESS MODE  IS DYNAMIC,
      *--> CHAve principal           (Ped+Tip.Regis+N.Item+Seq)-10 Bytes
                                  RECORD KEY   IS NC-CHA,
      *--> Chave Tipo reg/ped/it/seq (Tip.Regis+Ped+N.Item+Seq)-10 Bytes
                                  ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS NC-CTP =
                                  NC-REX, NC-PEX, NC-NIS,
      *--> Chave tipo Reg/it/seq/ped (Tip.Regis+N.Item+Seq+Ped)-10 Bytes
                                  ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS NC-CRI =
                                  NC-REX, NC-NIS, NC-PEX,
                                  LOCK MODE    IS MANUAL
                                  WITH LOCK    ON RECORD,
                                  FILE STATUS  IS FS-OBP.

       FD          NOBPED         RECORD CONTAINS 142 CHARACTERS,
                                  RECORDING MODE IS VARIABLE.
      *--> REGistro de OBservacoes de Pedidos
       01  NREG-OBP.
      *--> CHAve das observacoes do pedido - 10 Bytes
           05 NC-CHA.
      *--> numero do PEdido alfanumerico - X - 3 Bytes
              10 NC-PEX.
      *--> numero do PEDido
                 15 NC-PED        PIC IS 9(07)                   COMP-X.
      *--> tipo de REgistro alfanumerico - X - 1 Byte
              10 NC-REX.
      *--> tipo de REGistro de observacoes
                 15 NC-REG        PIC IS 9(02)                   COMP-X.
      *--> tipo de REGistro - OK/validos
                    88 NC-REGOK                   VALUES ARE 01 THRU 03.
      *--> tipo de REGistro - OBserv.do Pedido/nota
                    88 NC-REGOBP                  VALUE IS 01.
      *--> tipo de REGistro - GRaDes de quantidade
                    88 NC-REGGRD                  VALUE IS 02.
      *--> tipo de REGistro - VOLumes
                    88 NC-REGVOL                  VALUE IS 03.
      * -------------------------------------------------------------- *
      *--> Numero do Item/Sequencial - X - 6 Bytes
              10 NC-NIS.
      *--> Numero do ITem
                 15 NC-NIT        PIC IS 9(03)                   COMP-X.
      * -------------------------------------------------------------- *
      *--> SEQuencial
                 15 NC-SEQ        PIC IS 9(09)                   COMP-X.
      *--> Tabela de DEscricoes das observacoes - 132 Bytes
           05 NC-TDE.
      * -------------------------------------------------------------- *
      *--> DEScricao da observacao
              10 NC-DES           PIC IS X(132).
      * -------------------------------------------------------------- *
      *--> Tipo de Registro: 06 NC-REGCVC - Cliente Venda a Consumidor
      *--> Redefinicao p/tipo de registro: 06 - NC-REGCVC
           05 NC-R06    REDEFINES NC-TDE.
      * -------------------------------------------------------------- *
      *--> redefinicao p/Dados do COnsumidor - 107 Bytes
              10 NC-DCO.
      *--> DEscricao do Consumidor
                 15 NC-DEC        PIC IS X(32).
      *--> Inscricao EStadual da empresa/cart.identidade (pessoa fisica)
                 15 NC-IES        PIC IS X(15).
      *--> CIDade
                 15 NC-CID        PIC IS X(20).
      *--> ESTado
                 15 NC-EST        PIC IS X(02).
      *--> ENDereco
                 15 NC-END        PIC IS X(34).
      *--> CEP consumidor
                 15 NC-CEP        PIC IS 9(08)                   COMP-X.
      * -------------------------------------------------------------- *
      *--> Filler Cliente venda a Consumidor (low-values)
              10 NC-FCC           PIC IS X(25).
...
...
...

Good Bye,

---------------------------------------------------
  Rovani Marcelo Rech
  Rech Informatica Ltda - Novo Hamburgo/RS - Brazil
  www.rech.com.br - rovani@rech.com.br

Portuguese:

D�vida sobre Relativity performance.

Eu estou projetando um novo sistema de faturamento e tenho algumas
d�vidas sobre como projet�-lo para ter a melhor performance poss�vel
com o Relativity.

Eu gostaria de saber se a chave principal e as chaves alternativas s�o
utilizadas nos filtros de tipos de registros?

Eu tenho 1 arquivo que tem diversos tipos de registros, que s�o
definidos pelo segundo campo da chave principal. Em uma chave
alternativa este campo est� definido como o primeiro campo da chave
alternativa. Caso as chaves sejam utilizadas como filtro de tipos de
registros, eu gostaria de saber se o Relativity � "inteligente" o
suficiente para utilizar a chave alternativa para agilizar o filtro de

Sent via Deja.com
http://www.deja.com/
```

#### ↳ Re: Doubt on Relativity performance-Record Types

- **From:** "Tom Morrison" <t.morrison@liant.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-02T13:15:44-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<kUDe6.29$lA2.797@client>`
- **References:** `<95eiva$1d0$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Dear Rovani Marcelo Rech:

Rech Informatica Ltda/Brazil <rovani@rech.com.br> wrote in message
news:95eiva$1d0$1@nnrp1.deja.com...
> Dear Friends,
>
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> used in the filters of record types?

It is typical, but not necessary, that the record type field is contained in
the primary key, as well as most/all of the alternate keys.  Relativity can
work with the record type field (or fields - there can be any number of
fields used to define the record type) anywhere in the portion of the record
area shared by the various record types.


>
>    I have 1 file that has several record types, that are defined for
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> a record type.

The example file (which I have deleted) should work very nicely.  After
importing the file definition, define the record types using NC-REGOBP,
NC-REGGRD, and NC-REGVOL, then define the tables.  It is not necessary, or
even desirable, to include a column for NC-REG (or NC-REX) in your tables,
since the value is implied by the record type used to define the table.
Relativity will choose the best key depending on the SQL statement (usually
based upon the WHERE clause, the JOIN conditions, and the ORDER BY clause),
automatically supplying the value for NC-REG based upon the record type used
for the table.

>
> For instance:
>
>
>            SELECT  NOBPED         ASSIGN       TO NW-DRIOBP,
[snip]

Best regards,
Tom Morrison               www.liant.com
Liant Software Corporation
```

<a id="t-9c01e28474"></a>
## Thread: New Syntax? — 2 msgs

### New Syntax?

- **From:** "Sam" <Music98@Mindspring.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-02T13:43:09-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95f2ni$ve1$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I've been seeing references to level 78 entries, and the usage of COMP-X.
Are these part of the COBOL 2000 standards, and what are their significance?

--Sam
```

#### ↳ Re: New Syntax?

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-02T14:33:26-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95f5kf$f66$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<95f2ni$ve1$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
No, neither are part of the 200x Standard - but are in common usage for
several workstation COBOL compilers.

78 levels are "constants"  - The draft Standard includes a new attribute
called "CONSTANT" which is similar - but not identical.

The draft Standard introduces new usages BINARY-CHAR BINARY-SHORT
BINARY-LONG - which take *no* PICTURE clause (as COMP-X does) - but defines
a "true binary" field.
```

```
1) Can a MicroFocus Cobol program, compiled in a HP-UX environment, be
run in a Sun Solaris (Unix) environment, without further compilation?

2) What is CobRun for ?

3) Does MicroFocus Cobol compiler generate an executable (.exe?) or
rather generates an "to-be-interpreted" file ?

4) In order to be able to run a MicroFocus Cobol application (developed
on HP-UX) in a Sun Solaris environment, do we need any MicroFocus Cobol
software ? Which one ?

http://www.deja.com/
```

```
Jimmy, Calgary AB

rcarvalho1474@my-deja.com wrote:

> We are not acquainted with Cobol. So, we have a few questions.
> 1) Can a MicroFocus Cobol program, compiled in a HP-UX environment, be
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> http://www.deja.com/
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Cobol environment

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-02-04T09:22:29+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A7D1F55.E8D7A6B6@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<95fd6r$r3a$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <3A7B76E7.5CF08A6B@home.com>`

```
> rcarvalho1474@my-deja.com wrote:
> 
> > We are not acquainted with Cobol. So, we have a few questions.
> > 1) Can a MicroFocus Cobol program, compiled in a HP-UX environment, be
> > run in a Sun Solaris (Unix) environment, without further compilation?

Any MF Cobol can produce .int (intermediate) compiled programs that are
bytecode (ie not object code) that can be run by any MF run-time of the
same version.  If you compiled to .int on HP-UX then these will run on
Solaris with the appropriate run-time (application server).

The MF bytecodes were first used in around 1978.  Much the same was done
with UCSD Pascal, RM and ACCU-Cobol and, lately, Java.

The .int's are not optimised in any way.  .gnt's (generated) are usually
portable to the same processor class.  eg Compiled .gnt on SCO Unixware
will run under the Intel Linux application server (same CPU class).

> > 2) What is CobRun for ?

To run the .int and/or .gnt byte coded programs.  (just like 'javac'
will compile and 'java' will execute).

> > 3) Does MicroFocus Cobol compiler generate an executable (.exe?) or
> > rather generates an "to-be-interpreted" file ?

Yes.

You can produce .int (to be interpreted), .gnt (generated but relies on
run-time support) or stand-alone executables.  On DOS or Windows these
would be .EXE.  On DOS/Windows/OS/2 you can bury .int into the .EXE so
it acts like a stand-alone but is interpreted with the interpreter in
the .EXE.

On Unix it is rather harder to build a stand-alone executable.

> > 4) In order to be able to run a MicroFocus Cobol application (developed
> > on HP-UX) in a Sun Solaris environment, do we need any MicroFocus Cobol
> > software ? Which one ?

You need the appropripriate run-time or application server for Solaris,
or whatever they call it.  Their web-site has a list.  I understand that
Sun also has its version of MF so check the Sun site too.
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol environment

- **From:** "R.Freitag" <r.freitag@netcologne.de>
- **Date:** 2001-02-03T13:48:53+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A7BFE35.F17AE8FD@netcologne.de>`
- **References:** `<95fd6r$r3a$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
rcarvalho1474@my-deja.com wrote:
> 
> We are not acquainted with Cobol. So, we have a few questions.
> 1) Can a MicroFocus Cobol program, compiled in a HP-UX environment, be
> run in a Sun Solaris (Unix) environment, without further compilation?

Maybe. If the program does not use the Micro Focus enhancements, (i.e.
it is standard Cobol) it should be compiled by any standard compiler. So
you can compile it in a HP-environment. If it runs in a Solaris
environment? I don't know.
> 
> 2) What is CobRun for ?
> 
> 3) Does MicroFocus Cobol compiler generate an executable (.exe?) or
> rather generates an "to-be-interpreted" file ?

My produces executables for DOS. They run in a Windows environment.
> 
```

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95g9sc$22de$1@newssvr06-en0.news.prodigy.com>`

#### ↳ Re: tables

- **From:** "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-04T02:07:31+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<DP2f6.359$d_1.51722@newsread1.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<20010203013353.02235.00000299@ng-ft1.aol.com>`

```
Consider:

01  MYTABLE.
     05  MYLIST OCCURS 50 (table stuff goes here).
          10  MYABBR     PIC X(2).
          10  MYNAME     PIC X(25).

That you've loaded with data.

The code that you might use to find a name associated with an
abbreviation:

PERFORM VARYING CTR FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL CTR > 50
     IF TEST = MYABBR(CTR)
         MOVE MYNAME(CTR) TO MYRESULT
         END-IF
     END-PERFORM

Now, if you add "INDEXED BY CTR" in the area above marked (table stuff
goes here) you can replace the above code with:

SEARCH MYTABLE VARYING CTR
     WHEN TEST = MYABBR(CTR)
          MOVE MYNAME(CTR) TO MYRESULT
          END-SEARCH

and the above code sequentially examines every entry in the table,
just like the longhand way above.

Now here's the really neat part: If you can guarantee the table is in
order, replace (table stuff goes here) with "ASCENDING KEY IS MYABBR
INDEXED BY CTR"

Then you can use:

SEARCH ALL MYTABLE
     WHEN TEST = MYABBR(CTR)
     etc.

But the search is a binary search instead of sequential and is, for
large tables, very much faster.

Be sure to populate the array with HIGH-VALUES before loading your
data.

It is left as an exercise for the reader to fill in the exotic stuff
(AT END, AND, index item, DESCENDING, etc.)
```

#### ↳ Re: tables

- **From:** jacksleight@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2001-02-04T19:56:38+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95kc5m$b6f$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<20010203013353.02235.00000299@ng-ft1.aol.com>`

```
Hi Deek,

Maybe this will help:

Suppose you have a bookcase with 5 shelves each capable of holding 20
books of equal size. Each book contains 35 chapters.

If I asked you to read chapter 23 of the 15th book on the 3rd shelf,
how would you go about it?

After 3 weeks of this you complained that the requests were too
wordy, taking too much time. So we defined the bookcase as follows:

                BKCASE
                   SHELVES        OCCURS  5
                      BOOKS       OCCURS 20
                        CHAPTERS  OCCURS 35

And we came up with this notation for me to communicate to you what
chapter in a book I wanted:

         BKCASE(3,15,23)

We just made a "table" out of a bookcase and designed a way to get at
any element in the table/bookcase.

You can test your understanding of the concept by answering these
questions:

What if I had 3 bookcases?
What would I have to do to get at a specific word in a chapter?

Please note that in COBOL all the elements within an occurs group must
be the same # of bytes, whereas the words on a line of a book or the
line on a page do not.

Hope this helps you get started anyway. Keep on truckin. Jack




In article <20010203013353.02235.00000299@ng-ft1.aol.com>,
  deek40@aol.com (Deek40) wrote:

or type

> Thanks Deek

http://www.deja.com/
```

```
In article <20010203013353.02235.00000299@ng-ft1.aol.com>,
  deek40@aol.com (Deek40) wrote:
> Yuck i dont understand tables I have 3 books and they all use the
same program
> involving state abbreviations.  Can someone please point me to a site
or type
> up something to give me a hand?  I would really appreciate it.
> Thanks Deek

Hi:

Sorry to keep bringing up the Olden Days, but, in the Olden Days,
IBM and the other manufacturers provided their customers with
things like record-layout and memory-layout forms. These were
nothing more than large pages with little boxes each of which
represented a single memory location.

The reason I mention this is because it may make things easier for you
to be able to visualize the contents of working-storage and record
layouts.

If you were to use one of these forms to lay out a state table,
it would look like this (without the boxes which I cannot reproduce
here)ALAKAZARASCACZCOCTDEDCFLGAGUHI. . . Where each state is a
two-character field and the whole is redefined as a 52(?)-field
table. It might also contain the abbreviations for places like
Guam and the Canadian provinces.

Thanks

Tony Dilworth


Sent via Deja.com
http://www.deja.com/
```

#### ↳ Re: tables

- **From:** lcccpiasabird@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2001-02-07T16:35:17+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95rtg5$kpp$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<20010203013353.02235.00000299@ng-ft1.aol.com>`

```
To understand a table think of rows and colums.

Draw a diagram showing the rows and columns and it will be easier to
understand.  The problem is that columns are inside of rows when you
set up the table.  In actuality all the data is one byte after another.
You are defining a table by saying inside of a certain variable, there
is a field which occurs a certain number of times, and if there is a
second level inside of that there is a field that occurs a certain
nunber of times inside that field. Example:

row1(col1,col2), row2(col1,col2)

or:

r1c1,r1c2,r1c3,r2c1,r2c2,r2c3

one way to test this is to build a table and put a default value in
each element and that way you know if you got it right.
```

## Thread: Please Help me — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Please Help me

- **From:** "Paulo Pinto" <ct1ete@mail.telepac.pt>
- **Date:** 2001-02-03T16:36:21
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95hcfc$jbm$1@venus.telepac.pt>`

```
Hi all,

I have some applications developed under COBOL Workbench 3.4.3 and I would
like to change to a new product because it's going to be discontinued.

1) What do you recommend me ??
2) I've been told about NETEXPRESS (what do you think?)

If I choose Netexpress do I need to change the programs ??

How about the runtime costs ?????

Thanks in advance

Paulo Pinto
ct1ete@mail.telepac.pt
```

#### ↳ Re: Please Help me

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-02-04T09:34:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A7D2214.DFCF3CD6@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<95hcfc$jbm$1@venus.telepac.pt>`

```
> 
> I have some applications developed under COBOL Workbench 3.4.3 and I would
> like to change to a new product because it's going to be discontinued.

That doesn't mean that your copy will stop working.

> 1) What do you recommend me ??
> 2) I've been told about NETEXPRESS (what do you think?)
> 
> If I choose Netexpress do I need to change the programs ??

3.4 creates programs for DOS, OS/2 (16 bit) and Windows 3.x.  Netexpress
is for Windows 95 and NT (and later) only.  It will produce console apps
that look like DOS, but if yopu need DOS or Win 3.x then you won't
change.

Windows API programs will require conversion.  DOS based ADIS programs
will only require changes if you have used DOS specific routines (such
as CALL x"84") or other DOSisms.
 
> How about the runtime costs ?????

With 3.4 you can produce free distributable programs as long as you
follow the list in the book.

With NetExpress you must buy run-times or application-servers (or
whatever they call them.  There is supposed to be a way around this if
you ask nicely and are a small company.
```

#### ↳ Re: Please help me

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-04T03:44:32+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A7CD16C.3348B6CF@home.com>`
- **References:** `<95hcfc$jbm$2@venus.telepac.pt>`

```


Paulo Pinto wrote:

> I have some applications developed under Microfocus Cobol Workbench 3.4.3
> and I would like to change to a new product because it's going to be
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> What about runtime costs ?

Paulo,

You can get very specific answers to all the questions you have asked  from
Merant (Micro Focus).

I'm guessing, with a reasonable degree of confidence, that you wont need to
change programs. Set up a project in NetExpress and just do a re-compile. (I
don't know 3.4.3. - I only used the DOS 3.1 and now use NetExpress for OO).

If it aint broke why fix it ? So let me ask you a question(s). Are you
satisfied with 3.4.3 - or are you still in need of technical support, which
you wont get when  they discontinue the product. If that's not the reason and
it does the job - why upgrade. Or, are you considering an upgrade because of
new features. If so, what are those new features you are looking for.
Assuming you were thinking about 'windowing' - then that gives a different
slant. Please spell out your specification as to what you want a new compiler
to give you.
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu Cobol V4 and Win95

- **From:** thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)

```
I think you mean that you dloaded v3 - anyway, \FSC\PCOBOL32 is not in
your path.  Most times this is caused by ME or Windwos 98 not
processing Autoexec.bat.  On Win95 it's caused by the installer
messing up the path.  Manually add the above to the path and you
should be fine.
```

```
> MOVE BIG-ITEM TO W-RESTO-DISPLAY


Or redefine Big-Item as:

   05  Big-Split REDEFINES Big-Item.
       07  Big-Integer    PIC 9(9).
       07  Big-Fraction   PIC V99.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Using MOVE to Truncate is Counter-Intuitive (was: Re: Wrong calculation in NetExpress v3.0)

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@aiservices.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-03T21:33:12-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95if88$rl3$1@news.hitter.net>`
- **References:** `<94ruqp$kq2$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <951cp1$3pr$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk> <953nd0$jo6$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <95483a$cd3$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Bill, I was stunned by your comment! I have never seen or
heard anyone mention anything like it before. (That means
I think it deserves more comment! It also means I had to
think about for a few days!) ;-)

Never would I have considered using MOVE in a way that
would normally be considered an error. Showing a sales
total as $995,000.00 one month and $  1,000.00 the next
after an increase of $  6,000.00, is something I would never
want to happen. Because we seek to avoid a loss of digits,
using a MOVE to cause such a loss is counter-intutive.

Nonetheless, it is interesting. The following program may
not be the best or most efficient example; but it is simple.
In one MOVE with three destinations, a date in one format
is converted, truncated, and re-formatted into another. The
trick is in the pictures.

      $set flag"ans85" flagas"s"
       identification division.
       program-id. c-i.
       data division.
       working-storage section.
       01 a-date-yyyymmdd comp pic 9(8).
       01 formatted-date.
         03 formatted-month pic z9pp.
         03 pic x value "/".
         03 formatted-day pic 99.
         03 pic x value "/".
         03 formatted-year pic 9(4)p(4).
       procedure division.
       begin.
           compute a-date-yyyymmdd =
               function numval ( function current-date (1:8) )
           move a-date-yyyymmdd to
              formatted-month
              formatted-day
              formatted-year
           display formatted-date
           stop run
           .
       end program c-i.
------------------
Rick Smith

William M. Klein wrote in message <95483a$cd3$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>...
[...]
>
>If what you really want is TRUNCATION, then do the arithmetic into a field
>"large enough" to hold the full value - then MOVE it to the field where you
>want truncation.  (MOVE "defines" truncation - arithmetic does NOT)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Using MOVE to Truncate is Counter-Intuitive (was: Re: Wrong calculation in NetExpress v3.0)

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@aiservices.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-07T12:46:59-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95s1ut$2knu$1@news.hitter.net>`
- **References:** `<94ruqp$kq2$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <951cp1$3pr$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk> <953nd0$jo6$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <95483a$cd3$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net> <95if88$rl3$1@news.hitter.net> <FNnf6.188$4t3.38555@newsread2.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`

```

Jerry P wrote in message ...
>Eeek!

You can make the same comment about the results of
performance testing.

I ran 5 tests. Each was run 400 000 times. Two served
as baselines for comparison. Three to test the performance
using different types as the sending field for the MOVE.
The results are given in DOS timer ticks, approximately
55 ms per tick.

Baseline static 3
Baseline move 18
Binary 75
Packed 58
Display 50

Baseline static consists of three moves from numeric
display to numeric-edited (one field) and numeric display
(two fields).

Baseline move consists of, first, moving numeric binary
to numeric display then the three moves of Baseline
static.

The Binary, Packed, and Display tests each used a
single move statement with three destinations; as in
the following:
            move binary-yyyymmdd to
               formatted-month
               formatted-day
               formatted-year
Each of the receiving fields was scaled using P in the
picture clause.

A comparison of the results of the Basline tests shows
how expensive radix conversion can be on a machine
with no hardware assist.

Comparing the single moves to the Baselines shows
how expensive using P in the picture clause can be.

Perhaps the reason truncation with MOVE is seldom
discussed is because there is seldom any benefit.

I used the MF 3.2.24 compiler for testing.
The complete test program follows:
---------
       identification division.
       program-id. date-t.
      * Tests speed for formatting a date using
      * different methods and data types.
      * MS-DOS specific - accesses BIOS data for timing
       data division.
       working-storage section.
       01 binary-yyyymmdd binary pic 9(8).
       01 pd-yyyymmdd packed-decimal pic 9(9).
       01 disp-yyyymmdd pic 9(8).
       01 redefines disp-yyyymmdd.
         03 rd-year pic 9(4).
         03 rd-month pic 99.
         03 rd-day pic 99.
       01 formatted-date.
         03 formatted-month pic z9pp.
         03 pic x value "/".
         03 formatted-day pic 99.
         03 pic x value "/".
         03 formatted-year pic 9(4)p(4).
       01 redefines formatted-date.
         03 rd-formatted-month pic z9.
         03 pic x.
         03 rd-formatted-day pic 99.
         03 pic x.
         03 rd-formatted-year pic 9(4).
       01 test-type pic x(10).
       01 test-time pic zzz9.
       01 start-of-test comp-5 pic x(4).
       01 end-of-test comp-5 pic x(4).
       01 number-of-tests comp-5 pic 9(8) value 400000.
       01 address-of-timer.
         03 timer-location comp-5 pic xx value h"006c".
         03 timer-segment comp-5 pic xx value h"0040".
       01 pointer-to-timer redefines address-of-timer pointer.
       linkage section.
       01 bios-timer comp-5 pic x(4).
       procedure division.
       begin.
           set address of bios-timer to pointer-to-timer
           compute binary-yyyymmdd =
               function numval ( function current-date (1:8) )
           move binary-yyyymmdd to disp-yyyymmdd
           move binary-yyyymmdd to pd-yyyymmdd
           perform baseline-tests
           perform single-move-tests
           stop run
           .
       baseline-tests.
           move bios-timer to start-of-test
           perform baseline-static number-of-tests times
           move bios-timer to end-of-test
           compute test-time = end-of-test - start-of-test
           move "Baseline:" to test-type
           display test-type test-time space formatted-date

           move bios-timer to start-of-test
           perform baseline-move number-of-tests times
           move bios-timer to end-of-test
           compute test-time = end-of-test - start-of-test
           move "BL Move:" to test-type
           display test-type test-time space formatted-date
           .
       baseline-static.
           move rd-month to rd-formatted-month
           move rd-day to rd-formatted-day
           move rd-year to rd-formatted-year
           .
       baseline-move.
           move binary-yyyymmdd to disp-yyyymmdd
           move rd-month to rd-formatted-month
           move rd-day to rd-formatted-day
           move rd-year to rd-formatted-year
           .
       single-move-tests.
           move bios-timer to start-of-test
           perform single-move-binary number-of-tests times
           move bios-timer to end-of-test
           compute test-time = end-of-test - start-of-test
           move "Binary:" to test-type
           display test-type test-time space formatted-date

           move bios-timer to start-of-test
           perform single-move-pd number-of-tests times
           move bios-timer to end-of-test
           compute test-time = end-of-test - start-of-test
           move "Packed:" to test-type
           display test-type test-time space formatted-date

           move bios-timer to start-of-test
           perform single-move-disp number-of-tests times
           move bios-timer to end-of-test
           compute test-time = end-of-test - start-of-test
           move "Display:" to test-type
           display test-type test-time space formatted-date
           .
       single-move-binary.
           move binary-yyyymmdd to
              formatted-month
              formatted-day
              formatted-year
           .
       single-move-pd.
           move pd-yyyymmdd to
              formatted-month
              formatted-day
              formatted-year
           .
       single-move-disp.
           move disp-yyyymmdd to
              formatted-month
              formatted-day
              formatted-year
           .
       end program date-t.
------------------
Rick Smith
```

## Thread: New GUI/BUI on Legacy Business Logic??? — 2 this month / 4 total

_Topics: [`COBOL's future, legacy, and obsolescence`](topics.md#future) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

_Thread started [2001-01](2001-01.md#t-2330089288)._

#### ↳ Re: New GUI/BUI on Legacy Business Logic???

- **From:** Jeff Brown <jdbrown@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-03T23:59:48-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A7CE1C4.BA9FA06C@bellsouth.net>`
- **References:** `<lKw76.110$mo2.16881@news1.news.adelphia.net>`

```
Merant has two products that may help you. EnterpriseLink allows you to take
existing Cobol programs and put an HTML/Java interface on them. They have just
release another product which will be integrated to EL, it is called Component
Generator. This product allows you to trace a transaction across multiple
programs and identify all the pieces of code used for it. It will then allow you
to extract the pieces of code used and componentize them as well as provide a
new web-based interfaces.  The catch right now is that it only runs under CICS
at this time.

Xanteus wrote:

> I am currently exploring methodologies/products for implementing a GUI
> and/or BUI front-end over existing Cobol Business Logic.
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> Thanks in Advance.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: New GUI/BUI on Legacy Business Logic???

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-04T07:42:10+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A7D090A.92033F14@home.com>`
- **References:** `<lKw76.110$mo2.16881@news1.news.adelphia.net> <3A7CE1C4.BA9FA06C@bellsouth.net>`

```
Xanteus,

I can't comment on your particular environment - but I do use GUIs in NetExpress
(desktop version if you like). ...."pains or glory stories....." - pain, but not too
much glory, although I'm getting there. Three options (1) call APIs (2) GUI classes
or (3) Dialog System. I use # 2 and certainly initial pain  - if you are absolutely
new to windowing. Additionally, NetExpress now includes links to Java, but I haven't
used them.

I'm sure you can probably use all three, (four), or if you want to use 'vanilla
flavour' let Flexus SP2 or Norcom Screen IO handle the 'funny stuff' leaving you
with traditional structured programs. They both are geared to COBOL.

Just to throw it into the mix, Fujitsu POWERCOBOL offers a very interesting GUI
tool, generating (as the equivalent of int code), the necessaries for GUI-ing and
methods.

A factor to remember - GUIs are not and probably never will be part of the COBOL
standards, so if it is built into NetExpress or Fujitsu, it is NON-PORTABLE. That's
not the case if you are using SP2/ScreenIO or are invoking VB, Java etc. - they
represent stand alones. There are COBOL developers successfully using the
tools/languages I've mentioned.

Why I really responded to this was I picked up on Jeff's reference to a Component
Generator - this is the type of thing, as I understand it, that POWERCOBOL does.
It makes sense to pursue, probably outside of COBOL, a Component Generator.

When the person describing the concept to me, said in effect, ".... the Generator
lets you drag and drop controls from a toolbar, or a reference to a database, gets
the associated properties, plus the associated method procedure for each control.
etc......".   Then my ears pricked up when he continued,  "...... and you can do
this in 6 seconds as opposed to some 20 minutes in COBOL or C ......."

So before you jump - certainly pursue this Component Generator concept - important -
it can be language independent.


Jimmy, Calgary AB

…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
```

## Thread: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL problem — 4 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu POWERCOBOL problem

- **From:** "Steve Millward" <mindhawk@beeb.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-04T11:55:59
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95jg24$1thv$1@news.beeb.net>`

```
I am using Fujitsu Powercobol for the first time.
I am trying to pass data, held in tables, to other sheets.
The project consists of a main sheet (where the files are opened and table
data loaded), then other sheet linking off the main one.
I want to pass the data from the tables to these other sheets, it does say
in the manual to use the EXTERNAL clause, but doesn't really describe HOW to
do it.

I hope someone can help me....

Thanks

Steve
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL problem

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2001-02-04T20:30:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<95jg24$1thv$1@news.beeb.net>`

```

writes:

>I am trying to pass data, held in tables, to other sheets.
>The project consists of a main sheet (where the files are opened and table
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I am not 100% certain , but I think that this method would require that 
the table be defined in all sheets where the data is used.
The closest sample that I can think of is the PRTDLL sample program
I got from adtools.com.  The program was not a PowerCOBOL program,
but it does demonstrate use of EXTERNALly defined files.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL problem

- **From:** "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-05T01:56:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<uLnf6.181$4t3.37916@newsread2.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<95jg24$1thv$1@news.beeb.net> <20010204153057.08093.00001561@nso-fe.aol.com>`

```

"Sff5ky" <sff5ky@aol.comxxx123
>
> I am not 100% certain , but I think that this method would require
that
> the table be defined in all sheets where the data is used.

Exactly right.

However you define your stuff in one module (EXE or DLL) your stuff
must be defined in exactly the same way (same variable names,
everything) in the other modules. Each definition should be an '01'
level and include the word EXTERNAL. For example:

01  MYSTUFF EXTERNAL.
   02  MYSTUFF-TABLE OCCURS 50.
      05  MYSTUFF-STATE      PIC X(2).
      05  MYSTUFF-NAME       PIC X(30).

If you intend to use MYSTUFF in multiple procedures within a form, in
addition to EXTERNAL you should include GLOBAL. So, think of EXTERNAL
as a multi-module directive for GLOBAL.
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL problem

- **From:** "Paulo Vieira" <pvieira@emporsoft.pt>
- **Date:** 2001-02-05T10:56:47
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95m12m$q62$1@venus.telepac.pt>`
- **References:** `<95jg24$1thv$1@news.beeb.net>`

```
First, you should define your tables in EVERY form that is going to use
them, EXACTLY the same way (a nice aproach is to write them as separate
modules and #INCLUDE them in the apropriate WORKING-STORAGE of those forms).
As you didn't state the version of PowerCobol, beware that with version 5,
you'll need to specify a Cobol85 compiled module instead of a OO-Cobol
module, or the EXTERNAL clause will give you an error when compiling. Two
more considerations:
a) Note the #INCLUDE statement instead of COPY, as the later is targeted for
the compiler and the first for the PreProcessor of PowerCobol.
b) Besides EXTERNAL, the data items must be defined as GLOBAL as in:
01        MyTable IS GLOBAL EXTERNAL.
    05    MyElement    OCCURS ...(whatever)
     10    FirstField        PIC ...
     10    SeconfField    PIC ...

regards,
Paulo Vieira
http://www.emporsoft.pt

news:95jg24$1thv$1@news.beeb.net...

…[3 more quoted lines elided]…

to
```

## Thread: Calling Crystal Reports (7) from Acucobol — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Calling Crystal Reports (7) from Acucobol

- **From:** ddm_214@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2001-02-07T20:51:30+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95scgd$44i$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
I am successfully executing many of the basic calls to the
Crystal Reports Engine DLL (CRPE32.DLL).  I am having a problem
creating a data structure to use to logon to an SQL database (Oracle
8i in this case).

The Crystal Documentation says that the following structure needs to be
passed:
   struct PELogOnInfo
	{WORD structSize;
	char ServerName [PE_SERVERNAME_LEN];
	char DatabaseName [PE_DATABASENAME_LEN];
	char UserID [PE_USERID_LEN];
	char Password [PE_PASSWORD_LEN];};

My feeble attempts to code this in COBOL have all resulted in error
messages from crystal (error code 538) saying that the structure is
no good.
 one such attempt:
  01  logon-structure.
      05  logon-structure-size  pic s9(4) comp-5.
      05  logon-server-name     pic x(8).
      05  logon-db-name         pic x(8).
      05  logon-user-id         pic x(8).
      05  logon-password        pic x(8).

I am terminating each char field with nulls (as I have done in other
successful calls) and setting the structure size before the call.

I've also tried a version with the length of each field prefixing the
actual field itself, i.e

 01  logon-structure.
      05  logon-structure-size  pic s9(4) comp-5.
      05  logon-server-name-len pic s9(4) comp-5.
      05  logon-server-name     pic x(8).
      05  logon-db-name-len     pic s9(4) comp-5.
      05  logon-db-name         etc etc ....


The Acucobol help / docs are not much help in this situation.

Does anyone have any insights on how to do this?
Thanks


Sent via Deja.com
http://www.deja.com/
```

```
> Crystal Reports Engine DLL (CRPE32.DLL).  I am having a problem
```

##### ↳ ↳ RE: Calling Crystal Reports (7) from Acucobol

- **From:** ddm_214@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2001-02-09T18:49:58+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<961e4i$dio$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<95scgd$44i$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <95tkb6$rcl$1@lola.ctv.es>`

```
I'll supply the answer to my own question since (with Seagate Softwares
excellent support) I have resolved the problem and got the program to
work.

The logon structure is a fixed structure size, not as I had assumed a
variable size.  The size of the structures is defined in the
GLOBAL32.BAS file that is included with the API.  So the answer was
right there all the time.  Here is how the structure actually looks
when coded correctly:


  01 WS-LOGON-STRUCT.
     05  WS-LOGON-LEN  PIC S9(04) COMP-5 VALUE 514.
     05  WS-SERVER     PIC X(128).
     05  WS-DB         PIC X(128).
     05  WS-USER       PIC X(128).
     05  WS-PASS       PIC X(128).

the GLOBAL32.BAS has the correct information of all the API structures.



Sent via Deja.com
http://www.deja.com/
```

```
You can turn "off" processing of the autoexec.bat - make sure it's
not.

Feel free to email me your autoexec.bat - sometimes the 3.0 installer
corrupts the path later and while it is in there, it's not in effect.
To tell for sure, open a dos prompt and type PATH and see if
C:\FSC\PCOBOL32 is in the path.
```

#### ↳ Re: need help. tables

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-02-06T11:41:37+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A7FE2F1.57469509@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<95m7c4$kia$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
kfederigh01@hotmail.com wrote:
> 
> writing a program putting two tables and got to validate it.  i did
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>     05 month occurs 12 times  pic 99.

It appears that you want to have 12 values (percentages), one for each
month.  Each of these percentages is to two digits.  You seem to be
trying to predefine the percentages as '1', '2', '1', etc but these are
only _single_ digits.

      WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
      01  Monthly-Percentages          VALUE '010201000203090501010100'.
          05  Monthly-Percentage       PIC 99 OCCURS 12.

This has 24 bytes of digits divided up into 12 groups of two digits each
such that Monthly-Percentage(1) has a value of 01 and
Monthly-Percentage(7) has a value of 09.

Either that or you only filled out the first 6 in the value statement
above.

What you _probably_ want to do is to have a month number in range 1 ..
12 and to calcualate the percentage and then store this result in the
table.  As in:

        COMPUTE Monthly-Percentage(Month-Number) ROUNDED =
                         ( Month-Sales - Month-Cost ) / Month-Sales *
100

(or however you think the percentage should be).
```

#### ↳ Re: need help. tables

- **From:** lcccpiasabird@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2001-02-07T15:53:14+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95rr14$iao$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<95m7c4$kia$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
If you want to put the average in a field based on the value of the
month ie(1,2,10,12), then you just do this:

01 MONTH-AVERAGE-TABEL.
   05 Month-average occurs 12 times pic 9(03) value zero.

then move something there

03 compute Month-average (1) = salesman-1-total (1) / month-total(1).

where you totaled up the total for the salesman and the total sales for
the month.  You didnt really say what the percentage was a percentage
of so I made it up.  If month is a field and all three items are in a
table with 12 occurrances you could replace the number 1 with the
variable month!  I would guess a salesman has a quota normally and you
would compare that, or the sales team has a quota and you compare that.


In article <95m7c4$kia$1@nnrp1.deja.com>,
  kfederigh01@hotmail.com wrote:
> writing a program putting two tables and got to validate it.  i did
> the first table and validated it. im having problems with the 2 table.
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>


Sent via Deja.com

```

## Thread: Net Express — 3 msgs

### Net Express

- **From:** alan_beecher@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2001-02-06T05:37:55+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95o2jj$bn5$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
I am currently rewriting two major Dos applications using sp2 and MF
16-bit compiler 3.4.23.  If I switch to a 32 bit compiler, will it have
to be Net Express?  I believe they are discontinuing support for the 4.0
compiler.  When I finish the rewrite with the 16 bit, will I be able to
recompile and go?  How about data files, are they compatible with both
16 and 32 bit programs?  I am used to compiling gnts and putting them in
a lbr which makes them easy to handle.  I believe that is not possible
with NE, everything is an exe, right?  I would appreciate comments on
these and other obstacles I face.

Alan Beecher


Sent via Deja.com
http://www.deja.com/
```

#### ↳ Re: Net Express

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-02-06T20:39:56+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A80611C.B48CA4F1@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<95o2jj$bn5$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
alan_beecher@my-deja.com wrote:
> 
> I am currently rewriting two major Dos applications using sp2 and MF
> 16-bit compiler 3.4.23.  If I switch to a 32 bit compiler, will it have
> to be Net Express?  

As long as you haven't used MF specific items, such as called routines
"CBL_.. or "PC_.. etc then it should be OK to go to any compiler that
you can get SP2 for.  

There are a few MFisms such as DE-EDITing that are MF specific too.

> I believe they are discontinuing support for the 4.0 compiler.  
> When I finish the rewrite with the 16 bit, will I be able to
> recompile and go? 

Just about, or you could take it to Unix/Linux in a day or so.

>  How about data files, are they compatible with both
> 16 and 32 bit programs?  

Yes.

> I am used to compiling gnts and putting them in
> a lbr which makes them easy to handle.  I believe that is not possible
> with NE, everything is an exe, right?  I would appreciate comments on
> these and other obstacles I face.

If you built .EXEs under 3.4 (or 4.x) and checked the list in the manual
you can distribute free.  With Net Express there is no such mechanism -
you require licenced application servers for every distribution on a
per-user basis.  There is some concession for small developers if you
ask nicely.
```

#### ↳ Re: Net Express

- **From:** "sandman" <vyjssm@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-08T10:21:18+08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<95svri$kun@netnews.hinet.net>`
- **References:** `<95o2jj$bn5$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```

<alan_beecher@my-deja.com> wrote in message
news:95o2jj$bn5$1@nnrp1.deja.com...
> I am currently rewriting two major Dos applications using sp2 and MF
> 16-bit compiler 3.4.23.  If I switch to a 32 bit compiler, will it have
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> Alan Beecher

If you work on window system, I don't think NetExpress is a good solution.
Fujitsu cobol is a much better choice.  I have been using Micro Focus cobol
from V2.2, to netexpress 3.0.   Before netexpress, it is protected by a
keypro,  so you can install on you desktop, notebook, and still get mobility
just by carrying keypro around.   NetExpress is protected by a softwarekey,
so everything is tied to one machine.  To change machine, you need to
uninstall and reinstall with the 'permission' from MicroFocus. Here is the
biggest problem, if you crash your system,  you got no chance to uninstall
first,  you will get a painful process with microfocus that make you feel
like a thief.  Anyway, my suggestion is that don't choose NetExpress, choose
Fujitsu cobol.

jason
```

```
seems there's some default code pages (ebcdic 500
I think) taken from somewhere ?

signed (against RACF) on the MVS...

  "Lucas, Todd" <Todd.Lucas@C-Cubed.net> wrote:
> On the MVS side, you could use the following
TCP/IP entry points.  They are
> documented in the "IBM TCP/IP for MVS -
Application Programming Interface
> Reference, V3R2" manual.  I don't think they
are documented in any of the OS/390
> manuals, though they are still supported as our
products use them (currently
> running OS/390 V2R7).
>
> CALL  EZACIC04,(CIFIELDS, CILENGTH),VL
*Convert MVS EBCDIC to PC ASCII
> CALL  EZACIC05,(CIENTRY, CILENCVT),VL
*Convert PC ASCII to MVS EBCDIC
```

- **Message-ID:** `<95reu1$38c$1@talia.mad.ttd.net>`

#### ↳ Re: RMKF format

- **From:** Vadim Maslov <vadik-nws@siber.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-09T06:20:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A838C4B.58AF9EF8@siber.com>`
- **References:** `<95reu1$38c$1@talia.mad.ttd.net>`

```
Siber Systems has a reader for RMKF data files.
It is written in C++ and does not depend on Cobol system.

You can use it to read Cobol data files into a C++ program.
Also we have a number of converters based on these readers.

In addition to RMKF we have readers for MF, FSC, Acu
and other Cobol data files.

More info at http://www.siber.com/sct/datafile/

Regards,
Vadim Maslov

MisterX wrote:

```

```
statement starts.  For your comparison, make sure you review the "WITH TEST" clause
of this command.
```

```
"Stefan Meyer" <meyerst@my-deja.com> wrote in message
news:95r0op$tbl$1@nnrp1.deja.com...
> Donald,
> I'll comment inline...
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>
<SNIP REST>

I am using Fujitsu, so the code might vary a bit, but here is a sample OOP
subroutine.  I just typed this in as a sample, but it should show you the
basics.  I am going to presume that you know how to do the same sorts of
things with subroutines, and I am sure that someone here can show you yhe
differences for MF.

       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. "USER-CODE".
       AUTHOR. D. L. Tees.
       SECURITY.
           Copyright (C), 2001, Donald L Tees.
      * Notes:
      * SAMPLE "calling" PROGRAM FOR OOP.
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       CONFIGURATION SECTION.
       REPOSITORY.
           CLASS DISP2005.
       SOURCE-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
       OBJECT-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
       SPECIAL-NAMES.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
       FILE-CONTROL.
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       77 OBJECT-INSTANCE-ONE      USAGE OBJECT REFERENCE DISP2005.
       77 OBJECT-INSTANCE-TWO      USAGE OBJECT REFERENCE DISP2005.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       MAIN-LINE.
      * FIRST, YOU HAVE TO CREATE THE OBJECTS
           INVOKE DISP2005 "NEW" RETURNING OBJECT-INSTANCE-ONE.
           INVOKE DISP2005 "NEW" RETURNING OBJECT-INSTANCE-TWO.
      * USING THE INSTANCE IS THE SAME AS A SUBROUTINE
           INVOKE OBJECT-INSTANCE-ONE "HELLO-WORLD".
           INVOKE OBJECT-INSTANCE-ONE "HELLO-SOMEONE"
                USING "DONALD".
      * HOWEVER, YOU CAN ALSO SET PROPERTIES
      * THIS CALL WILL LOOK THE SAME AS THE "hello-someone" using
           MOVE "DONALD" TO PERSON-NAME OF OBJECT-INSTANCE-TWO.
           INVOKE OBJECT-INSTANCE-TWO "HELLO-WORLD".
      * AFTER USING THEM, YOU HAVE TO DESTROY THEM
           SET OBJECT-INSTANCE-ONE TO NULL.
           SET OBJECT-INSTANCE-TWO TO NULL.
           GOBACK.


THIS IS THE OBJECT PROGRAM.

        CLASS-ID. DISP2005 INHERITS FJBASE.
        ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
        CONFIGURATION SECTION.
        REPOSITORY.
                CLASS FJBASE.
        OBJECT.
        ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
        INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.
        DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
        WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       * DATA COMMON TO ALL METHODS
        01 PERSON-NAME          PICTURE X(10) VALUE
                                SPACE PROPERTY.
        PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       * THE METHODS GO HERE.
*********************************************************
        METHOD-ID. HELLO-WORLD.
        DATA DIVISION.
        WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
        LINKAGE SECTION.
        PROCEDURE DIVISION.
        MAINLINE.
                IF PERSON-NAME IS EQUAL TO SPACE
                        DISPLAY "HELLO WORLD"
                ELSE
                        DISPLAY "HELLO " PERSON-NAME.
                EXIT METHOD.
        END METHOD HELLO-WORLD.

*********************************************************
        METHOD-ID. HELLO-SOMEONE.
        DATA DIVISION.
        WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
        LINKAGE SECTION.
        77 NAME-INPUT           PICTURE X(10).
        PROCEDURE DIVISION USING NAME-INPUT.
        MAINLINE.
                MOVE NAME-INPUT TO PERSON-NAME.
                INVOKE SELF "HELLO-WORLD".
                EXIT METHOD.
        END METHOD HELLO-SOMEONE.
        END OBJECT.
        END CLASS DISP2005.

That should give you the basic structure.  I have some actual code that is
in beta test right now.  Here is the "file object" code for one of the
files. Notice that the file stuff is all at the top so is global to all
methods.

******* File level objects
*******************************************************************
       CLASS-ID. DISP2010 INHERITS FJBASE.
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       CONFIGURATION SECTION.
       REPOSITORY.
           CLASS DISP2005
           CLASS FJBASE.
       OBJECT.
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.
           SELECT PRODUCT-FILE ASSIGN TO DPROD-ID
               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
               ACCESS MODE IS DYNAMIC
               LOCK MODE IS EXCLUSIVE
               FILE STATUS IS DPROD-STATUS
               RECORD KEY IS DPROD-NUMBER.
           SELECT CONTROL-FILE ASSIGN TO CONTROL-ID
               ORGANIZATION IS RELATIVE
               LOCK MODE IS EXCLUSIVE
               ACCESS IS RANDOM
               RELATIVE KEY IS CONTROL-KEY
               FILE STATUS IS CONTROL-STATUS.
           SELECT CUSTOMER-FILE ASSIGN TO DCUST-ID
               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
               ACCESS MODE IS DYNAMIC
               LOCK MODE IS EXCLUSIVE
               FILE STATUS IS DCUST-STATUS
               RECORD KEY IS DCUST-KEY
               ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS DCUST-ALPHA
                   WITH DUPLICATES.
           SELECT TRUCK-TYPE-FILE ASSIGN TO TTYPE-ID
               ORGANIZATION IS RELATIVE
               FILE STATUS TTYPE-STATUS
               RELATIVE KEY IS TTYPE-KEY
               LOCK MODE EXCLUSIVE
               ACCESS IS DYNAMIC.
           SELECT OLD-TRUCK-TYPE-FILE ASSIGN TO OLD-TTYPE-ID
               ORGANIZATION IS RELATIVE
               FILE STATUS OLD-TTYPE-STATUS
               RELATIVE KEY IS OLD-TTYPE-KEY
               LOCK MODE EXCLUSIVE
               ACCESS IS DYNAMIC.
           SELECT DTRK-FILE ASSIGN TO DTRK-IDENT
               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
               ACCESS MODE IS DYNAMIC
               LOCK MODE IS EXCLUSIVE
               FILE STATUS IS DTRK-STATUS
               RECORD KEY IS DTRK-KEY
               ALTERNATE KEY IS
                   DTRK-SHOW, DTRK-DATE
                   DTRK-IN, DTRK-KEY
               ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS DTRK-DISPATCH-KEY
                   WITH DUPLICATES.
           SELECT CARD-FILE ASSIGN TO CARD-IDENT
              ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
               ACCESS MODE IS DYNAMIC
               FILE STATUS IS CARD-STATUS
               RECORD KEY IS CARD-NUMBER OF CARD-RECORD
               ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS CARD-TRUCK WITH DUPLICATES
               ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS CARD-CUSTOMER WITH DUPLICATES.
           SELECT TICKET-FILE ASSIGN TO TICKET-IDENT
               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
               ACCESS IS DYNAMIC
               FILE STATUS IS TICKET-STATUS
               RECORD KEY IS T-TICKET-NUMBER.
       DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
       FD PRODUCT-FILE.
       01 PRODUCT-RECORD.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\PRODUCT.COBOL".
       FD CONTROL-FILE.
       01 CONTROL-RECORD.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\CONTROL.COBOL".
       FD CUSTOMER-FILE.
       01 CUSTOMER-RECORD.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\CUSTOMER.COBOL".
       FD TRUCK-TYPE-FILE.
       01 TRUCK-TYPE-RECORD.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\TTYPE.COBOL".
       FD OLD-TRUCK-TYPE-FILE.
       01 OLD-TRUCK-TYPE-RECORD.
           02 TRUCK-TYPE-ENTRY     OCCURS 20 TIMES.
              04 TRUCK-TYPE-DESC PICTURE X(5).
       FD DTRK-FILE.
       01 TRUCK-RECORD.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\TRUCK.COBOL".
       FD CARD-FILE.
       01 CARD-RECORD.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\CARD.COBOL".
       FD TICKET-FILE.
       01 TICKET-RECORD.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\TICKET.COBOL".
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       77 PROGRAM-NAME-ABORT   PICTURE X(8) VALUE SPACE PROPERTY.
       77 VERSION-A                            PICTURE 9 VALUE ZERO
PROPERTY.
       77 VERSION-B                            PICTURE 99 VALUE ZERO
PROPERTY.
       77 SOURCE-MODULE               PICTURE X(45) VALUE SPACE PROPERTY.
       77 DPROD-STATUS                   PICTURE XX VALUE SPACE.
       77 DPROD-ID                             PICTURE X(80) VALUE SPACE.
       77 CONTROL-STATUS           PICTURE XX VALUE SPACE.
       77 CONTROL-KEY              PICTURE 9(5) VALUE ZERO.
       77 CONTROL-ID               PICTURE X(80) VALUE SPACE.
       77 DCUST-STATUS           PICTURE XX VALUE SPACE.
       77 DCUST-ID                 PICTURE X(80) VALUE SPACE.
       77 TTYPE-STATUS           PICTURE XX VALUE SPACE.
       77 TTYPE-ID               PICTURE X(80) VALUE SPACE.
       77 TTYPE-KEY                PICTURE 99.
       77 OLD-TTYPE-STATUS         PICTURE XX VALUE SPACE.
       77 OLD-TTYPE-ID             PICTURE X(80) VALUE SPACE.
       77 OLD-TTYPE-KEY            PICTURE 99.
       77 DTRK-STATUS           PICTURE XX VALUE SPACE.
       77 DTRK-IDENT               PICTURE X(80) VALUE SPACE.
       77 CARD-IDENT               PICTURE X(80) VALUE SPACE.
       77 CARD-STATUS           PICTURE XX VALUE SPACE.
       77 WINDOW-NAME              PICTURE X(8) VALUE SPACE.
       77 TICKET-STATUS           PICTURE XX VALUE SPACE.
       77 TICKET-IDENT             PICTURE X(80) VALUE SPACE.
       01 WINDOW-RECORD-DATA.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\WINDOW.COBOL".
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\COBF-INF.COBOL".
       01 MISCSE02-PARAMETERS.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\MISCSE02.COBOL".
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
*******************************************************************
       METHOD-ID. ABORT-METHOD.
       DATA DIVISION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01 BASE-OBJECT          USAGE OBJECT REFERENCE DISP2005.
       LINKAGE SECTION.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       MAINLINE.
           INVOKE DISP2005 "NEW" RETURNING BASE-OBJECT.
           MOVE "DISPATCH" TO THIS-SYSTEM-NAME OF BASE-OBJECT.
           MOVE PROGRAM-NAME-ABORT TO PROGRAM-NAME OF BASE-OBJECT.
           MOVE VERSION-A OF SELF TO VERSION-A OF BASE-OBJECT.
           MOVE VERSION-B OF SELF TO VERSION-B OF BASE-OBJECT.
           MOVE SOURCE-MODULE OF SELF TO SOURCE-MODULE OF BASE-OBJECT.
           INVOKE BASE-OBJECT "ABORT-METHOD".
           SET BASE-OBJECT TO NULL.
           EXIT METHOD.
       END METHOD ABORT-METHOD.
*******************************************************************
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\PRODFILE.CBL".
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\CONTFILE.CBL".
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\CUSTFILE.CBL".
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\TTYPFILE.CBL".
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\TRKFILE.CBL".
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\CARDFILE.CBL".
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\TICKET.CBL".
*******************************************************************
       END OBJECT.
       END CLASS DISP2010.


And one of the copy libraries for one of the files.

       METHOD-ID. OPEN-LOCK-DPROD.
       DATA DIVISION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01 BASE-OBJECT              USAGE OBJECT REFERENCE DISP2005.
       01 SCREEN-OBJECT            USAGE OBJECT REFERENCE DISP2005.
       01 LOCAL-KEYSTROKE          PICTURE S999.
       01 LOCK-TIME                PICTURE 99.
       01 FILE-ID-AREA             PICTURE X(80).
       01 BADFILE-AREA             PICTURE X(80).
       01 MESSAGE-AREA.
           02 DISPLAY-PART         PICTURE X(40).
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X(512).
       LINKAGE SECTION.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       DECLARATIVES.
       TROUBLE SECTION.
           USE AFTER ERROR PROCEDURE ON PRODUCT-FILE.
       CHECK-STATUS.
           CONTINUE.
       END DECLARATIVES.
       MAINLINE.
           IF DPROD-ID IS EQUAL TO SPACE
               INVOKE DISP2005 "NEW" RETURNING BASE-OBJECT
               MOVE "DISPATCH" TO THIS-SYSTEM-NAME OF BASE-OBJECT
               MOVE "G" TO PARAMETER-TYPE OF BASE-OBJECT
               MOVE "DPRODID" TO PARAMETER-NAME OF BASE-OBJECT
               MOVE SYSTEM-PARAMETER OF BASE-OBJECT TO DPROD-ID
               SET BASE-OBJECT TO NULL.
           MOVE ZERO TO LOCK-TIME.
           MOVE "99" TO DPROD-STATUS.
           PERFORM OPEN-THE-FILE UNTIL
               DPROD-STATUS IS EQUAL TO "00".
           IF SCREEN-OBJECT IS NOT EQUAL TO NULL
               INVOKE SCREEN-OBJECT "CLOSE-ERROR-MESSAGE"
               SET SCREEN-OBJECT TO NULL.
           EXIT METHOD.

       OPEN-THE-FILE.
           OPEN I-O PRODUCT-FILE.
           IF DPROD-STATUS IS EQUAL TO "35"
               PERFORM CREATE-NEW-FILE.
           IF DPROD-STATUS IS EQUAL TO "39"
               PERFORM ATTEMPT-TO-RECOVER
           ELSE
           IF DPROD-STATUS IS EQUAL TO "93"
               PERFORM SHOW-THE-LOCK
           ELSE
           IF DPROD-STATUS IS EQUAL TO "41"
               DISPLAY "PRODUCT status --> " DPROD-STATUS
                       " (Open attempt on open file)"
               INVOKE SELF "ABORT-METHOD"
           ELSE
           IF DPROD-STATUS IS NOT EQUAL TO "00"
               DISPLAY "Open PRODUCT status --> " DPROD-STATUS
               INVOKE SELF "ABORT-METHOD".

       SHOW-THE-LOCK.
           IF LOCK-TIME IS EQUAL TO ZERO
               INVOKE DISP2005 "NEW" RETURNING SCREEN-OBJECT
               MOVE "DPRODID" TO LOCK-CODE OF SCREEN-OBJECT
               MOVE "Product file" TO LOCK-NAME OF SCREEN-OBJECT
               MOVE DPROD-STATUS TO LOCK-STATUS OF SCREEN-OBJECT
               INVOKE SCREEN-OBJECT "SHOW-LOCK"
               ADD 1 TO LOCK-TIME
           ELSE
           IF LOCK-TIME IS LESS THAN 60
               MOVE DPROD-STATUS TO LOCK-STATUS OF SCREEN-OBJECT
               INVOKE SCREEN-OBJECT "SHOW-LOCK"
               ADD 1 TO LOCK-TIME
           ELSE
               DISPLAY "Product file lock time exceeded"
               INVOKE SELF "ABORT-METHOD".

       ATTEMPT-TO-RECOVER.
           INVOKE DISP2005 "NEW" RETURNING SCREEN-OBJECT.
           MOVE "Product file" TO LOCK-NAME OF SCREEN-OBJECT.
           MOVE DPROD-STATUS TO LOCK-STATUS OF SCREEN-OBJECT.
           INVOKE SCREEN-OBJECT "ASK-RECOVER".
           MOVE SCREEN-KEYCODE OF SCREEN-OBJECT
               TO LOCAL-KEYSTROKE.
           IF LOCAL-KEYSTROKE IS EQUAL TO 500
               PERFORM ATTEMPT-TO-RECOVER-TWO
           ELSE
           IF LOCAL-KEYSTROKE IS EQUAL TO 501
               SET SCREEN-OBJECT TO NULL
               PERFORM CREATE-NEW-FILE
           ELSE
               SET SCREEN-OBJECT TO NULL
               INVOKE SELF "ABORT-METHOD".

       ATTEMPT-TO-RECOVER-TWO.
           MOVE LOW-VALUES TO COBF-INF.
           MOVE "C:\WILLMACK\PRODUCT.ERROR" TO COBF-INPUT-FILENAME.
           DISPLAY "Deleting old error file C:\WILLMACK\PRODUCT.ERROR".
           CALL "COB_FILE_DELETE" USING BY REFERENCE COBF-INF.
           MOVE "C:\WILLMACK\PRODUCT.ERROR" TO COBF-INPUT-FILENAME.
           MOVE "C:\WILLMACK\PRODUCT.ERROR" TO BADFILE-AREA.
           MOVE DPROD-ID TO FILE-ID-AREA.
           DISPLAY "File id " FILE-ID-AREA.
           DISPLAY "Error file " BADFILE-AREA.
           DISPLAY "Starting recover".
           CALL "CFURCOV" USING BY REFERENCE FILE-ID-AREA
                                BY REFERENCE BADFILE-AREA
                                BY REFERENCE MESSAGE-AREA.
           INSPECT MESSAGE-AREA REPLACING ALL X'00' BY " ".
           DISPLAY DISPLAY-PART.
           DISPLAY "Doing reorganize".
           MOVE LOW-VALUES TO COBF-INF.
           MOVE "C:\WILLMACK\PRODUCT.ERROR1" TO COBF-INPUT-FILENAME.
           DISPLAY "Deleting old error file C:\WILLMACK\PRODUCT.ERROR1".
           CALL "COB_FILE_DELETE" USING BY REFERENCE COBF-INF.
           MOVE LOW-VALUES TO COBF-INF.
           MOVE DPROD-ID TO COBF-INPUT-FILENAME.
           MOVE "C:\WILLMACK\PRODUCT.ERROR1" TO COBF-OUTPUT-FILENAME.
           CALL "COB_FILE_REORGANIZE" USING BY REFERENCE COBF-INF.
           INSPECT MESSAGE-AREA REPLACING ALL X'00' BY " ".
           DISPLAY DISPLAY-PART.
           SET SCREEN-OBJECT TO NULL.

       CREATE-NEW-FILE.
           OPEN OUTPUT PRODUCT-FILE.
           CLOSE PRODUCT-FILE.
           OPEN I-O PRODUCT-FILE.

       END METHOD OPEN-LOCK-DPROD.
*******************************************************************
       METHOD-ID. CLOSE-DPROD.
       DATA DIVISION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       LINKAGE SECTION.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       MAINLINE.
           CLOSE PRODUCT-FILE.
           EXIT METHOD.
       END METHOD CLOSE-DPROD.
*******************************************************************
       METHOD-ID. PRODUCT-RECORD-UPDATE.
       DATA DIVISION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01 BASE-OBJECT              USAGE OBJECT REFERENCE DISP2005.
       LINKAGE SECTION.
       77 REQUEST-TYPE             PICTURE X.
       01 PRODUCT-RECORD-IN.
           COPY "\LIBRARY\COPIES\PRODUCT.COBOL".
       77 ERROR-CODE               PICTURE 9.
       77 READ-WRITE-FLAG          PICTURE X.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING REQUEST-TYPE
                             PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
                             ERROR-CODE
                             READ-WRITE-FLAG.
       MAINLINE.
           IF DPROD-ID IS EQUAL TO SPACE
               INVOKE DISP2005 "NEW" RETURNING BASE-OBJECT
               MOVE "DISPATCH" TO THIS-SYSTEM-NAME OF BASE-OBJECT
               MOVE "G" TO PARAMETER-TYPE OF BASE-OBJECT
               MOVE "DPRODID" TO PARAMETER-NAME OF BASE-OBJECT
               MOVE SYSTEM-PARAMETER OF BASE-OBJECT TO DPROD-ID
               SET BASE-OBJECT TO NULL.
           MOVE ZERO TO ERROR-CODE.
           MOVE "Y" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF (REQUEST-TYPE IS NOT EQUAL TO "1"
               AND
               REQUEST-TYPE IS NOT EQUAL TO "2"
               AND
               REQUEST-TYPE IS NOT EQUAL TO "4"
               AND
               REQUEST-TYPE IS NOT EQUAL TO "3")
                   INVOKE SELF "OPEN-LOCK-DPROD".
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "R" PERFORM DPROD-READ
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "4" PERFORM DPROD-READ
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "D" PERFORM DPROD-DELETE
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "U" PERFORM DPROD-REWRITE
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "X" PERFORM DPROD-X-DESC
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "Y" PERFORM DPROD-Y-TONS-ORDER
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "Z" PERFORM DPROD-Z-TONS-ORDER
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "L" PERFORM DPROD-OVER-UNDER-LOADS
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "C" PERFORM DPROD-CHANGE
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "V" PERFORM DPROD-REVERSE-CHANGE
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "N" PERFORM DPROD-CREATE-NEW
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "S" PERFORM DPROD-SET-ZERO
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "3" PERFORM DPROD-SET-ZERO
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "1" PERFORM DPROD-START-READ1
           ELSE
           IF REQUEST-TYPE = "2" PERFORM DPROD-READ-SEQUENTIAL
           ELSE
               DISPLAY "PRODDATA --> Illegal call"
               INVOKE SELF "ABORT-METHOD".
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD TO PRODUCT-RECORD-IN.
           IF (REQUEST-TYPE IS NOT EQUAL TO "1"
               AND
               REQUEST-TYPE IS NOT EQUAL TO "2"
               AND
               REQUEST-TYPE IS NOT EQUAL TO "4"
               AND
               REQUEST-TYPE IS NOT EQUAL TO "3")
                   INVOKE SELF "CLOSE-DPROD".
           EXIT METHOD.

       DPROD-READ.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
              MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG IS EQUAL TO "N"
               MOVE 3 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-DELETE.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
               MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "Y"
               PERFORM DPROD-DELETE-CONTINUE
           ELSE
               MOVE 5 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-DELETE-CONTINUE.
           DELETE PRODUCT-FILE RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE 5 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-REWRITE.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           REWRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "N"
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-X-DESC.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
              MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "Y"
              PERFORM DPROD-X-DESC-CONTINUE
           ELSE
               MOVE 3 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-X-DESC-CONTINUE.
           MOVE DPROD-DESC OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-DESC OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           MOVE DPROD-PLANT-NUMBER OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-PLANT-NUMBER OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           REWRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-Y-TONS-ORDER.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           MOVE "Y" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
               MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "Y"
              PERFORM DPROD-Y-TONS-ORDER-CONTINUE
           ELSE
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-Y-TONS-ORDER-CONTINUE.
           ADD DPROD-TONS-ORDER OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-TONS-ORDER OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           REWRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-Z-TONS-ORDER.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
              MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "Y"
              PERFORM DPROD-Z-TONS-ORDER-CONTINUE
           ELSE
               MOVE 3 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-Z-TONS-ORDER-CONTINUE.
           SUBTRACT DPROD-TONS-ORDER OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               FROM DPROD-TONS-ORDER OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           REWRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-OVER-UNDER-LOADS.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           MOVE "Y" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
              MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "Y"
              PERFORM DPROD-OVER-UNDER-LOADS-CONT
           ELSE
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-OVER-UNDER-LOADS-CONT.
           ADD DPROD-OVERLOADS OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-OVERLOADS OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           ADD DPROD-UNDERLOADS OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-UNDERLOADS OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           REWRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-CHANGE.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
                MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "Y"
               PERFORM DPROD-CHANGE-CONTINUE
           ELSE
               MOVE 3 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-CHANGE-CONTINUE.
           IF DPROD-LOADS-TODAY OF PRODUCT-RECORD IS EQUAL TO ZERO
               ADD 1 TO DPROD-LOADS-TODAY OF PRODUCT-RECORD
               MOVE DPROD-AVE-LOAD-TIME OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
                  TO DPROD-AVE-LOAD-TIME OF PRODUCT-RECORD
           ELSE
               MULTIPLY DPROD-AVE-LOAD-TIME OF PRODUCT-RECORD BY
                   DPROD-LOADS-TODAY OF PRODUCT-RECORD
                       GIVING DPROD-AVE-LOAD-TIME OF PRODUCT-RECORD
               ADD DPROD-AVE-LOAD-TIME OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
                   TO DPROD-AVE-LOAD-TIME OF PRODUCT-RECORD
               ADD 1 TO DPROD-LOADS-TODAY OF PRODUCT-RECORD
               DIVIDE DPROD-AVE-LOAD-TIME OF PRODUCT-RECORD
                 BY DPROD-LOADS-TODAY OF PRODUCT-RECORD
                 GIVING DPROD-AVE-LOAD-TIME OF PRODUCT-RECORD ROUNDED.
           ADD DPROD-UNDERLOADS OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-UNDERLOADS OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           ADD DPROD-OVERLOADS OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-OVERLOADS OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           ADD DPROD-TONS-FOB OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-TONS-FOB OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           ADD DPROD-TONS-DELIVER OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               TO DPROD-TONS-DELIVER OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           REWRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-REVERSE-CHANGE.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
               MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "Y"
               PERFORM DPROD-REVERSE-CHANGE-CONT
           ELSE
               MOVE 3 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-REVERSE-CHANGE-CONT.
           SUBTRACT 1 FROM DPROD-LOADS-TODAY OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           SUBTRACT DPROD-TONS-FOB OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               FROM DPROD-TONS-FOB OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           SUBTRACT DPROD-TONS-DELIVER OF PRODUCT-RECORD-IN
               FROM DPROD-TONS-DELIVER OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           REWRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-CREATE-NEW.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           WRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG = "N"
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-SET-ZERO.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE INVALID KEY
              MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG IS EQUAL TO "Y"
               PERFORM DPROD-SET-ZERO-CONTINUE.

       DPROD-SET-ZERO-CONTINUE.
           MOVE ALL "0" TO DPROD-DATA OF PRODUCT-RECORD.
           REWRITE PRODUCT-RECORD INVALID KEY
               MOVE 6 TO ERROR-CODE.

       DPROD-START-READ1.
           MOVE PRODUCT-RECORD-IN TO PRODUCT-RECORD.
           START PRODUCT-FILE
               KEY GREATER THAN DPROD-NUMBER OF PRODUCT-RECORD
                   INVALID KEY
                       MOVE 3 TO ERROR-CODE
                       MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.
           IF READ-WRITE-FLAG IS EQUAL TO "Y"
               READ PRODUCT-FILE NEXT RECORD
                   AT END
                       MOVE 3 TO ERROR-CODE
                       MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.

       DPROD-READ-SEQUENTIAL.
           READ PRODUCT-FILE NEXT RECORD
               AT END MOVE "N" TO READ-WRITE-FLAG.

       END METHOD PRODUCT-RECORD-UPDATE.
```
