<a id="t-beb765df65"></a>
## Thread: cobol in-state program — 12 msgs

### cobol in-state program

- **From:** alex <alexinaustin@bwn.net-3>
- **Date:** 2001-02-13T06:50:36-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3>`

```
does anybody know how to create a program that will maintain
its previous state.  i mean, that all variables will retain their values

between calls.  of course this implies that the code is resident???
```

#### ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** Joseph Kohler <joe_kohler@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-13T09:11:38-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A89409A.1963C7FC@yahoo.com>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3>`

```
alex wrote:
> 
> does anybody know how to create a program that will maintain
> its previous state.  i mean, that all variables will retain their values
> 
> between calls.  of course this implies that the code is resident???

Yes, you simply call the program as a dynamic call and all the working
storage maintains the state when called next.  To free the storage
occupied by the program issue a CANCEL call.  On an IBM Mainframe the
call statement must use a working-storage variable to hold the name of
the subprogram.

Joe

Joe
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-13T09:37:29-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96bkb3$5bh$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3> <3A89409A.1963C7FC@yahoo.com>`

```
On IBM mainframes, you do NOT need to use CALL identifier to get a dynamic
CALL - you may *also* use the DYNAM compiler option with CALL "literal".
HOWEVER, there are some environments (most notably CICS) where the use of
the DYNAM compiler option is not allowed.

FYI,  if you are looking for information related to the original question,
check your documentation (language reference manual and/or text book) for
the term "last used state".

NOTE: In addition to using CANCEL to "end" the last-used state, it is also
possible (in current but not older compilers) to put the INITIAL attribute
on the program-id paragraph.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** Joseph Kohler <joe_kohler@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-13T10:44:31-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A89565F.27732A7E@yahoo.com>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3> <3A89409A.1963C7FC@yahoo.com>`

```
Joseph Kohler wrote:
> 
> alex wrote:
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
> Joe

Alex,

Once the first call to a dynamically called module is
made the program will occupy a storage area and keep
it until the job ends.  If you just need different
programs to call a "black box" program, and they are
all within the same jobstream, a dynamically called
module should work.  If you need something running all
the time independent of a single jobstream then you
need a transaction monitor type program that can stay
active and wait for calls from other processes.

Joe
```

#### ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T12:14:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8A76C1.61B28F32@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3>`

```
alex wrote:
> 
> does anybody know how to create a program that will maintain
> its previous state.  i mean, that all variables will retain their values
> 
> between calls.  of course this implies that the code is resident???

If you have a routine that is CALLed several times from another program
(or from more than one other) then by default each CALL will find it in
the state it was when it did its last EXIT PROGRAM.

To change this so that it is reloaded to its initial state on a CALL it
should be CANCELed or it should have IS INITIAL PROGRAM in its
Program-Id paragraph.

So the answer to your question is "nothing".
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T00:41:26+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010213194126.24359.00000771@ng-mc1.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<3A8A76C1.61B28F32@Azonic.co.nz>`

```

Other posters have responded literally, with excellent comments.

An alternative to consider is to actually place the data you are interested in
preserving in the _calling_ program and provide addressability to such area(s)
via linkage section in the _called_ module. As long as the _calling_ module
survives its current invocation, the area(s) should stay 'static', even as you
bound in and out of the submodule that might manipulate the area(s) for you.  

This side steps the issue of dynamic/static call and the INITIAL attribute on
the submodule's PROGRAM-ID statement. ((CICS is doing this kind of thing with
getmain services and comareas, but you can do it too, ... as long as the
program that has the info in its working storage never exits, it's working
storage will survive, no matter how many submodules it invokes providing access
to the area(s).


Robert Rayhawk
rayhawk@alum.calberkeley.org
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** alex <alexinaustin@bwn.net-3>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T10:57:43-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8AB907.73C22263@bwn.net-3>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3> <3A8A76C1.61B28F32@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
Richard Plinston wrote:

> alex wrote:
> >
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
> So the answer to your question is "nothing".

let me restate this.  if I had a called program that increments a number and
returns it to the calling program, how do i preserve this value so that when
this program is called by ANOTHER program, I can increment the value
and return it???? .
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ( NA)
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T18:16:08+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<IXzi6.16555$Sl.737412@iad-read.news.verio.net>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3> <3A8A76C1.61B28F32@Azonic.co.nz> <3A8AB907.73C22263@bwn.net-3>`

```
In article <3A8AB907.73C22263@bwn.net-3>, alex  <alexinaustin@bwn.net-3> wrote:

[snippage]

>let me restate this.  if I had a called program that increments a number and
>returns it to the calling program, how do i preserve this value so that when
>this program is called by ANOTHER program, I can increment the value
>and return it???? .

Ow... consider the following scenario:

User01 runs PROGA which calls INVRTN.  INVRTN verifies a CUSTNO and
returns an INVNUM to PROGA to generate an invoice.

User02 runs PROGA which calls INVRTN.  INVRTN verifies another CUSTNO and
returns the next sequential INVNUM to PROGA to generate an invoice.

User03 runs PROGA... and a bug in another program brings the system down.

Everyone curses at the screen and goes for coffee.

Everyone returns.

User03 runs PROGA which calls INVRTN... how does INVRTN know what was the
last valid INVNUM?

Might be best to write data which are needed by different applications to
a file.

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T12:36:09-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96ej62$7vp$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3> <3A8A76C1.61B28F32@Azonic.co.nz> <3A8AB907.73C22263@bwn.net-3>`

```
You don't state in this "revision" whether the two separate CALLs are within
the same run-unit.

If your calling structure is:

  ProgA calls Keep-Prog (and returns to ProgA)
  ProgA calls ProgB which calls Keep-Prog (which returns to ProgB which
returns to Progra
  ProgA calls ProgC which calls Keep-Prog (which returns to ProgC which
returns to Progra

then (unless you CANCEL or use INITIAL attribute) Keep-Prog will be in "last
used state" each time you call it after the first program.

If, on the other hand, your structure is

 ProgA calls Keep-Prog (and returns to ProgA)
 ProgA does a "stop run" (or GoBack in systems that support it)
 ProgB calls Keep-Prog (and returns to ProgB)
 ProgB does a "stop run"

There there is *no* way to have Keep-Prog "remember" data values between
run-units.  You must "write" such values out to a file and then read them in
again.  No part of the COBOL data division "persists" across run-units.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

- **From:** Howard Brazee <howard@brazee.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T11:57:28-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8AD517.87DAAE1C@brazee.net>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3> <3A8A76C1.61B28F32@Azonic.co.nz> <3A8AB907.73C22263@bwn.net-3>`

```
alex wrote:

> let me restate this.  if I had a called program that increments a number and
> returns it to the calling program, how do i preserve this value so that when
> this program is called by ANOTHER program, I can increment the value
> and return it???? .

Write it to a file.   I used to do this with relative files.  The data entry
program would read record #1 and find where to write the next record.  It would
then add one to this number, and write record #1 out before writing the data
record.

The obvious concern here is record/file locking.  If two different programs want
to increment at the same time, the first program must open this file for update,
increment and close it before the second one can read it.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** alex <alexinaustin@bwn.net-3>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T06:22:02-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8BC9EA.CE945806@bwn.net-3>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3> <3A8A76C1.61B28F32@Azonic.co.nz> <3A8AB907.73C22263@bwn.net-3> <3A8AD517.87DAAE1C@brazee.net>`

```
here's my real problem.  i have coded a remote procedure call that's used
by many client programs.  This called procedure retrieves many DB2 rows
from many DB2 tables.  The result set is used by the client programs to cache
the values to be used locally on the desktop.

The problem is that each call to the remote procedure call by each client
program takes too damn long.  I was hoping for a way for the remote procedure
program to only read the DB2 tables one time, store the result set in working
storage and return the data from working storage only after the initial DB2
query.

Am I making any sense?????
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: cobol in-state program

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T16:53:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8C0B26.55752EE3@home.com>`
- **References:** `<3A892D9C.D94FACC@bwn.net-3> <3A8A76C1.61B28F32@Azonic.co.nz> <3A8AB907.73C22263@bwn.net-3> <3A8AD517.87DAAE1C@brazee.net> <3A8BC9EA.CE945806@bwn.net-3>`

```


alex wrote:

> here's my real problem.  i have coded a remote procedure call that's used
> by many client programs.  This called procedure retrieves many DB2 rows
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> Am I making any sense?????

Yes, you are. Now I recall back in Micro Focus Compuserve days, Michael Mattias
describing Relative Files being used as 'temporary working storage'. Isn't this
what you are after. Depending upon how you want to get at those rows and columns,
seems to me an easy way would be to create a temporary file ISAM, sequential or
relative - it doesn't  sound like an onerous task. Given that you are using DB2
maybe you want to stick with that approach - create a separate temporary DB2 file
that they can access, but on the latter I'm speculating as I don't use DB2.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

Hello Friends,

      I'm having problems when trying to unload a DLL. I've created a DLL in
delphi5 with multiple functions (entry points) and "load" it using a COBOL
procedure pointer as described in the on-line help:
...
77 EXCELDLL PROCEDURE-POINTER.
...
SET EXCELDLL TO ENTRY "RIXLEX.DLL".
...

    I belive that setting EXCELDLL to NULL will unload my dll. Is that
rigth?

    Do I have to use CANCEL in the DLL or in any of it's functions?

    I use Net Express 3.0. Please help me on solve this and thanks in
advance.

PS: Everything in the DLL work's fine, but I want to be sure about unloading
because the DLL execute Excel and it is very "crashable"!!!!

--------------------------------------------
Rovani Marcelo Rech
Rech Inform�tica Ltda / Brazil
rovani@rech.com.br

"Rovani Marcelo Rech" <rovani@rech.com.br> wrote in message
news:TQei6.211$dK.2699@newsfeed.slurp.net...
> Hello Friends,
>
>       I'm having problems when trying to unload a DLL. I've created a DLL
in
> delphi5 with multiple functions (entry points) and "load" it using a COBOL
> procedure pointer as described in the on-line help:
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> PS: Everything in the DLL work's fine, but I want to be sure about
unloading
> because the DLL execute Excel and it is very "crashable"!!!!
>

My experience is that you do need to CANCEL the .dll to remove it from
memory.  The problem I've had is that the memory is regained when you do
this.  If the .dll is large and you have several you need to load, the
memory usage can skyrocket.

Bill
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Can the COBOL function current-date be used in a CICS program

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-13T22:09:43-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96d0d9$r6l$1@slb0.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<dood6t0v3q3d2h12fd0v3d59r5s2efdrsf@4ax.com> <20010118092604.08836.00000019@ng-fv1.aol.com> <a14e6t8jjcg2sti4tb9h2ohf4cdtuh3skd@4ax.com> <3A89F8FF.D932AF2F@da-zoo.com>`

```
Just because others may have missed it,

If you have IBM COBOL for OS/390 & VM V2R2 (*not* V2R1), it is now (finally)
allowed in a CICS program to use ACCEPT from DAY/DATE/TIME.

This doesn't mean that you will always WANT to, but this is an "enhancement"
in the most recent version of COBOL.
```

<a id="t-32e324f5ba"></a>
## Thread: "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1 — 11 msgs

### "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1

- **From:** "Panos Zotos" <pzotos@syntax.gr>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T09:52:56+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`

```
Hi all,

Has anyone come accross the following situation?

We have ported an old character application written in Microsoft Cobol 2 to
Net Express 3.1 (same character interface) and installed it on a Windows NT
4 (sp5) server with Windows 98 SE clients.

Every data file was regenerated as MF"4" type with Net Express.

The problem we face is a quite strange one, as sometimes some of the records
that are being written on a file, are not finally stored to it! The flow of
the suspect part of the application is :

write a record to a file
print an invoice according to this record

Then in the end of the day we are trying to print the total invoices of the
day from the same file and some of them do not appear in the final list,
although they were printed in the first place ! This does not happen all the
time of course, but once or twice a week (which are enough to loose a lot of
money).

It seems that the server caches the new records before writing them to the
disk, and sometimes this write never happens. I have tried the Writethrough
complier directive but we did not see much improvement. Will Fileshare make
a difference or do you have anything else in mind?

Thank in advance for your help.

Panos Zotos
Athens, Greece
```

#### ↳ Re: "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1

- **From:** floydj@netins.net (Floyd Johnson)
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T13:41:31+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96e1ub$8ja$1@ins21.netins.net>`
- **References:** `<96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`

```
This might be one explanation:

Be sure that you close your files.  Part of the "close" operation is to
finish copying any data in the buffer to the file.  

The size of the buffer is equal to the "allocation unit" reported by the
"chkdsk" command.  Each write operation actually writes to the
buffer.    When the buffer is full, data is copied to the disk.

By shutting down a program - without executing a "close" - you will not
have copied the last set of records to the disk.

Floyd Johnson


Panos Zotos (pzotos@syntax.gr) wrote:
: Hi all,

: Has anyone come accross the following situation?

: We have ported an old character application written in Microsoft Cobol 2 to
: Net Express 3.1 (same character interface) and installed it on a Windows NT
: 4 (sp5) server with Windows 98 SE clients.

: Every data file was regenerated as MF"4" type with Net Express.

: The problem we face is a quite strange one, as sometimes some of the records
: that are being written on a file, are not finally stored to it! The flow of
: the suspect part of the application is :

: write a record to a file
: print an invoice according to this record

: Then in the end of the day we are trying to print the total invoices of the
: day from the same file and some of them do not appear in the final list,
: although they were printed in the first place ! This does not happen all the
: time of course, but once or twice a week (which are enough to loose a lot of
: money).

: It seems that the server caches the new records before writing them to the
: disk, and sometimes this write never happens. I have tried the Writethrough
: complier directive but we did not see much improvement. Will Fileshare make
: a difference or do you have anything else in mind?

: Thank in advance for your help.

: Panos Zotos
: Athens, Greece
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@aiservices.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T09:41:50-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96e5l5$28hp$1@news.hitter.net>`
- **References:** `<96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr> <96e1ub$8ja$1@ins21.netins.net>`

```
If the application terminates through a STOP RUN,
all open files are closed, implicitly. Open files in
a subprogram are closed, implicity, by CANCEL,
as well. See STOP RUN and CANCEL in your LRM.

While it is a good idea to close files, it is not required
by the standard.
------------------
Rick Smith

Floyd Johnson wrote in message <96e1ub$8ja$1@ins21.netins.net>...
>This might be one explanation:
>
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>Floyd Johnson
[...]
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1

- **From:** "Gary Susott" <susott@atta-boy.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T08:51:07-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<oPxi6.28538$JN5.240868@e420r-sjo3.usenetserver.com>`
- **References:** `<96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr> <96e1ub$8ja$1@ins21.netins.net> <96e5l5$28hp$1@news.hitter.net>`

```
While all the actions do allow for file closing before terminating the
application, an explicit close allows for checking for file status to insure
that the operation completed without error.   In response to this error,
another cause might be that file caching is turned on on either the server
or client or both and some other event might cause the eventual write to not
occurr.


Rick Smith <ricksmith@aiservices.com> wrote in message
news:96e5l5$28hp$1@news.hitter.net...
> If the application terminates through a STOP RUN,
> all open files are closed, implicitly. Open files in
…[25 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Panos Zotos" <pzotos@syntax.gr>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T10:02:52+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96g2c3$3vf$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`
- **References:** `<96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr> <96e1ub$8ja$1@ins21.netins.net> <96e5l5$28hp$1@news.hitter.net> <oPxi6.28538$JN5.240868@e420r-sjo3.usenetserver.com>`

```
Thank you for your answers guys!

It seems that caching is a main issue here and I really wonder how I can
stop caching from the server or the clients. I can have my application
writing directly to the file and suppress COBOL caching (through the
WRITETHRU setting in extfh.cfg) but I cannot suspect how to do it with Win
NT/98. If someone knows how to do that please let me know.

Thanks again.

Panos Zotos
Athens, Greece

"Gary Susott" <susott@atta-boy.com> wrote in message
news:oPxi6.28538$JN5.240868@e420r-sjo3.usenetserver.com...
> While all the actions do allow for file closing before terminating the
> application, an explicit close allows for checking for file status to
insure
> that the operation completed without error.   In response to this error,
> another cause might be that file caching is turned on on either the server
> or client or both and some other event might cause the eventual write to
not
> occurr.
>
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
> > >The size of the buffer is equal to the "allocation unit" reported by
the
> > >"chkdsk" command.  Each write operation actually writes to the
> > >buffer.    When the buffer is full, data is copied to the disk.
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc@bellsouth.net>

- **Message-ID:** `<lyxi6.3166$Uc6.86596@news2.atl>`
- **References:** `<96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`

```
"Panos Zotos" <pzotos@syntax.gr> wrote:
>
> We have ported an old character application written in Microsoft Cobol 2 to
> Net Express 3.1 (same character interface) and installed it on a Windows NT
> 4 (sp5) server with Windows 98 SE clients.

It is my gut feeling that when you finally pin down the cause, it will
be caused by Windows, rather than NetExpress.  Windows is the most
flaky, bug prone, unstable operating system I have had the 'pleasure'
to work with in over 32 years.  It is my opinion that this will not
change until users realize that reliability should be held as far more
important than glitz, and start demanding it.
```

Users did demand stability. He's running on an NT box.

Usually, the hierarchy of problem locations are (in reverse order):

Operating system failure.
Undiscovered compiler error.
Undiscovered program bug.
Operator error.

- **References:** `<96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr> <lyxi6.3166$Uc6.86596@news2.atl> <5BDi6.2636$wb6.238949@newsread1.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`

"Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com> wrote:
>
> "Judson McClendon" <judmc@bellsouth.net>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> Users did demand stability. He's running on an NT box.

If you think Windows NT is a 'stable' operating system, you must have
never worked in a mainframe environment!  My mainframe clients usually
only reboot their systems to update the operating system, and only
very rarely (perhaps once a year or less) see a system crash.  And
never, but never, does an errant programmer take the system down,
and that's with perhaps 40 or 50 programmers writing and testing code
all the time.  Of course, those systems only support some 5000 or more
simultaneous users, along with the programmers. :-)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com>

```

"Judson McClendon"

> If you think Windows NT is a 'stable' operating system, you must
have
> never worked in a mainframe environment!  My mainframe clients
usually
> only reboot their systems to update the operating system, and only
> very rarely (perhaps once a year or less) see a system crash.  And
> never, but never, does an errant programmer take the system down,
> and that's with perhaps 40 or 50 programmers writing and testing
code
> all the time.  Of course, those systems only support some 5000 or
more
> simultaneous users, along with the programmers. :-)

Sure I worked on a mainframe.

Shell. Humana. University of Houston data center. Public school HQ.
Started out on 1401s and 605s.

We had 'errant' programmers who could break (or at least s-t-r-e-t-ch)
magnetic tape! You can do magic things with channel programming.

But, in the PC world, NT is pretty stable. We rebooted our server just
yesterday for the first time in ever-so-long (to install the new UPS
software - go figure).
```

#### ↳ Re: "Lost" records in data file using Net Express 3.1

- **From:** "Hillel" <hss@larich.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T09:54:52-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<t8nrs7j6ma09a4@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr>`

```
Are you using Fileshare already?  If so, try stopping and
restarting Fileshare.  The records might not really be lost.

We had a similar problem, but we noticed that those
missing records could be accessed by a seldom-used
alternate key.  Fileshare somehow had marked the primary
key on those records so they would not be found, even though
we never read them with lock.  Restarting the Fileshare
process released the records.

---Hillel


Panos Zotos wrote in message <96ddde$1v7$1@usenet.otenet.gr>...
>Hi all,
>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>The problem we face is a quite strange one, as sometimes some of the
records
>that are being written on a file, are not finally stored to it! The flow of
>the suspect part of the application is :
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>although they were printed in the first place ! This does not happen all
the
>time of course, but once or twice a week (which are enough to loose a lot
of
>money).
>
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

Hello,
I have written a 32-bit Windows-Application with Netexpress-Cobol. Beside
some ISAM-Files also a Btrieve-File (V.6.15) is to be read on a NetWare-4.1
Server. In the programm I have defined the Btrieve-File with "SET
FILETYPE(5)" and I have linked the _btrv-module from Merant. On the netware
4.1-server. BTRIEVE.NLM and BSPXCOM.NLM are loaded. A 16-bit DOS-application
runs without problem when I first load brequest.exe. When I animate the
32-Bit application, a message-box appears with the following text:

Execution of the programm _BTRV has been interuppted........ The cause of
the interupt was: 114 Attempt to access item beyond bonds of memory.

Anyone have any ideas?

Many thanks

Thomas
```

## Thread: Fujitsu PowerCOBOL and Picture Objects — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu PowerCOBOL and Picture Objects

- **From:** Jeff Campbell <jcampbell@ins-msi.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T14:11:49+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8A8CD5.ED3FE85B@ins-msi.com>`

```
I am trying to use a third party control, Videosoft's VSVIEW7, on a
PowerCOBOL form. I am using the VSprinter to preview some text. This
works fine.

My problem is: I want to add a company logo to the preview pages. The
VSprinter has a DrawPicture method which uses as one of it's arguments
a reference to a Visual Basic Picture Object.

My question: How can I include a Picture Object with a bit map from
the logo .BMP file? I looked at the Image Control but it doesn't have
a Picture property. 

Hopefully, someone can point me in the right direction.

TIA,

Jeff Campbell
n8wxs@arrl.net
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu PowerCOBOL and Picture Objects

- **From:** "Paulo Vieira" <pvieira@emporsoft.pt>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T10:06:04
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96g9s6$m67$1@venus.telepac.pt>`
- **References:** `<3A8A8CD5.ED3FE85B@ins-msi.com>`

```
Hi,
I've never worked with Videosoft's controls, but "Visual Basic Picture
Object" is probably a handle and not a file name.
Let's get to both scenarios:
A) You need to pass the name of a picture file to the control: simply use
the "ImageName" property of the Image control. This property references the
file name which contains your picture.
B) You need to pass the memory address of the control: (this is only a
guess) do a CALL "POWCONVTOOLE" using your image control and returning a OLE
HANDLE, and then pass that handle to the VS control. As I said, this is
guess work because I never tried it before.
By the way, this is only possible with Version 5.
regards,
Paulo Vieira, emporsoft

"Jeff Campbell" <jcampbell@ins-msi.com> wrote in message
news:3A8A8CD5.ED3FE85B@ins-msi.com...

> PowerCOBOL form. I am using the VSprinter to preview some text. This
…[15 more quoted lines elided]…
> n8wxs@arrl.net
```

Well, I don't know how to do this. As far as I can tell the image
control
in PowerCOBOL doesn't export it's image. That is I don't see an "image"
property that could be handed to another control as in:

  move "image" of image-control to "image" of another-image-control.

Even if it did export it I doubt it's memory layout is the same as a
visual basic picture object. So I wouldn't be able to:

  invoke vsprinter1 "DrawPicture" image of image-control ...

because the DrawPicture method wants a picture object reference as
it's argument.

> By the way, this is only possible with Version 5.

Opps, I forgot to include that I am using version 5.

> regards,
> Paulo Vieira, emporsoft

Thanks again for taking the time to respond.

Jeff Campbell
n8wxs@arrl.net

_Topics: [`Meta: FAQs, group policy, charter`](topics.md#meta)_

### COBOL FAQ

- **From:** wmklein@ix.netcom.com (William M. Klein)
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T16:57:35+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol,news.answers,comp.answers,alt.answers
- **Message-ID:** `<computer-lang/cobol-faq_982169717@rtfm.mit.edu>`

```
Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month
Last-Modified: 2000/07/02
Version: 3.01
URL: http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Sunday, July 2, 2000

The COBOL FAQ has moved.  To view the most recent version, please check out the
web version at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send private
email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

The following is Appendix B.5 - Changes to create Version 3.01

 o Recorded new location of the FAQ
 o Updated some old (bad) web links
 o Added some new COBOL books (particularly those written or contributed to - by
frequent comp.lang.cobol submitters).
 o Added information about LegacyJ (PERCobol) and changed the company name from
"Synkronix" to "LegacyJ".
 o Updated information for changes in product and company names and addresses
where available
 o Updated the URLs for downloading the draft of the next COBOL Standard and
CobCy
 o Started the Samples/Example Section
 o Tried to get rid of most specific references to Windows/NT, Windows/95,
Windows/98, and Windows/2000.
 o Added links to Xinotech
 o Added information on Norcom and GUI ScreenIO
 o Added (back) information on Wang
 o Added (back) information on the Tiny-COBOL ('74 Standard) project
```

- **From:** atharmon@swbell.net (Tony Harmon)
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T17:26:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3a8abec2.411816350@news.swbell.net>`

```
All,

I'm looking for a IDE/Compiler/Debug package for personal use. I would
like to be able to code/debug batch, CICS or IMS programs, and test
them in this environment. Many years ago, my shop at the time, had
MicroFocus and we could test our CICS programs on the workstation, and
access our DB2/2 that was also on the workstation. That way we could
be productive as long as the power was still on. So network problems,
were someone else's problem. It used to be called MicroFocus
WorkBench, but I have no idea what it is called now. Could anyone
point me in the right direction?

Thanks,
Tony
```

#### ↳ Re: MicroFocus, Merant, whatever

- **From:** "Randy Zimmerman" <rzimmerman@wi.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T18:21:34+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<O0Ai6.4715$k%5.44009@typhoon.mw.mediaone.net>`
- **References:** `<3a8abec2.411816350@news.swbell.net>`

```
The product you are probably looking for is Mainframe Express from Merant.
CICS/DB2/ASM/PLI etc are now more tightly integrated with the IDE than in
with Micro Focus Workbench product.  You can go to this web-site for an
overview:
http://www.merant.com/products/microfocus/mainframe_express/factsheet.asp

Randy Zimmerman
MERANT

#### ↳ Re: MicroFocus, Merant, whatever

- **From:** Steve Burns <stephen.d.burns2@boeing.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T23:23:25+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8B136D.D9282107@boeing.com>`
- **References:** `<3a8abec2.411816350@news.swbell.net>`

```
Merant (and micro focus before it) has the distinction of being the
company with the best product and the worst marketing in the history of
computing.  It has alwasy been that way, and there is NO sign of any
change.  However, at least one merant person reads this group, so you
may well get contacted by them.  If you want some action though, send
mail to Steve Giovanelli, a merant sales guy in toronto.  He may not be
your sales rep, but he is certain to find out who is for you..... 
steve.giovanelli@merant.com

You definately want mainframe express 
(http://merant.com/products/microfocus/).  It is a nice product, but
rather pricey these days  (about $5000 per seat, depending on what all
you want).  (micro focus was pretty pricey, too, but not his pricey...) 
If you want something cheaper (with correspondingly cheaper bells and
whistles) try visual age for cobol from ibm, at about $1000 per seat. 
(http://www-4.ibm.com/software/ad/cobol/)

Good luck,

Tony Harmon wrote:
> 
> All,
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> Tony
```

A blunt statement, but sadly accurate - I find the Net Express product, IDE
etc. to be SUPERB - but copious examples and particularly support ???????
Why should I pay the annual fee to use Answerlab. Before my support expired,

left Newbury, "volunteered" his time to answer questions, because 'he
cared', but obviously just didn't have the time to go into explicit details.
I believe his 'unofficial' role has been taken on by David Sands.

And as I said some months ago to Steve Burns, I'm not about to swan off by
'plane to Merant User groups, Vancouver (now defunct I believe), or Toronto
- it's not like buying a ticket to go on a subway<G>. I believe Imperial
Oil, perhaps our biggest oil company, uses Mainframe Express - but seems
Toronto want to keep it hush-hush - so there's nobody else here in Calgary
I'm able to discuss COBOL with. (PS - last weekend I  saw a job advertised
in the Calgary Herald, where in addition to all the other goodies, Java,
HTML etc., they actually included C-O-B-O-L  experience as a desirable asset
! )

Well, I'm in the Great White North - so I'm sending this to Steve Giovanelli
to see what response I get. He may personally be very helpful, but I'm not
holding my breath. Steve G., you can write to me privately.

Jimmy, Calgary AB

>  However, at least one merant person reads this group, so you
> may well get contacted by them.  If you want some action though, send
…[29 more quoted lines elided]…
> > Tony
```

I've used both the 16-bit and 32-bit versions of Workbench for about 7 years
now, and I've found them very useful.   I just upgraded to Mainframe Express
and also got the Revolve product from Merant.

One word of warning........be sure what you buy is compatible with your
operating system.  Apparently Revolve doesn't work on Windows ME (although
every other piece of software I own written for Windows 98 does).  I haven't
found out about MFE yet.   I haven't had the chance to give it a try!

Good Luck

Dave

#### ↳ Re: Best way to convert overpunch character

- **From:** Rob Lec <RobLec@cinci-rr.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T19:10:48+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8C29C3.4DCF8C16@cinci-rr.com>`
- **References:** `<PQBi6.1971$C92.42089@news.pacbell.net>`

```
In Microfocus you can use "$set sign(ebcdic)" or something like that to
read the overpunch data and move it to SIGN SEPARATE fields.  This
results in a two-step conversion but I've done this recently and it
works.
-------------------------------
(To reply by email, change dash to dot in address)
```

## Thread: General question?? — 9 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### General question??

- **From:** Jeff Farkas <tazberjef@nospam.naxs.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T19:06:27-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MPG.14f4e78c8eedf7d4989694@news.naxs.com>`

```
Hi all..

  How hard would it be for a Mainframe COBOL programmer to move over to 
say, AS/400 COBOL??

TIA,,

Jeff.....
```

#### ↳ Re: General question??

- **From:** "Garvin T. Tabor" <gtabor@uswest.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-14T22:26:18-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A8B5A69.25A9297@uswest.net>`
- **References:** `<MPG.14f4e78c8eedf7d4989694@news.naxs.com>`

```
COBOL is COBOL; this is the language that set standards.  I have used it on
AS/400, Mainframe, HP3000 and PC/LAN (RPG, too).  To move over to AS/400
(one of my favorite machines) you need someone to show you how the
operating and storage system works.  An experienced programmer worth his
salt will make the transition easily enough.


Jeff Farkas wrote:

> Hi all..
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> Jeff.....
```

#### ↳ Re: General question??

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T08:18:39-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ZyRi6.3442$Uc6.113936@news2.atl>`
- **References:** `<MPG.14f4e78c8eedf7d4989694@news.naxs.com>`

```
"Jeff Farkas" <tazberjef@nospam.naxs.net> wrote:
>
> How hard would it be for a Mainframe COBOL programmer to move over
> to say, AS/400 COBOL??

In general, the primary differences between different brands of COBOL
is in user interface and database interface, because those things
have never been standardized in COBOL.  Beyond that, COBOL is pretty
much COBOL, except that some shops still use COBOL 74, some COBOL 85,
and some use extensions that are not yet in the official standard.
But any competent programmer can learn a new environment without too
much difficulty.  It's nothing to worry about. :-)
```

#### ↳ Re: General question??

- **From:** Terry Winchester <terrywin.nospam@pronetisp.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T16:42:48-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<82io8to1agkm7s083f62q49pgsp1cll432@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<MPG.14f4e78c8eedf7d4989694@news.naxs.com>`

```
On Wed, 14 Feb 2001 19:06:27 -0500, Jeff Farkas
<tazberjef@nospam.naxs.net> wrote:

>Hi all..
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>Jeff.....

Well, if *I* did it then YOU can do it ;-)
Here are the things you'll have to learn or adapt to:

	Display Files - AS/400 counterpart to BMS maps.
			The hardest part of learning to
			use COBOL on the AS/400. A couple
			of solid skeleton programs and
			your on your way...
	DB2 400       - The database is intrinsicly built
			within the operating system - OS/400. 
			Using it in COBOL is not difficult 
			at all (just standard stuff). 
	OS/400	      - You can be quickly overwhelmed
			by the number of available commands,
			but, you don't need to know many
			of them to just "program".
	CL Language   - AS/400's version of JCL - you'll
			love its ease of programming and
			its power.  The learning curve is
			is not too bad either.
	ILE	      - Integrated Language Environment.
			There are special considerations
			you must understand when coding
			complex interactive/batch business
			processes. The manuals are a solid
			place to helping you learn this.
 	SEU	      - Source Entry Utility - the editor 
			kinda looks like XEDIT but it's 
			*different*.  Not a big deal...

All-in-all, its a solid environment and FUN to work in.
If I'm lucky, I'll stay on this platform till I retire :)

Good Luck,
Terry

<remove "nospam" if you have more questions>
```

- **From:** Jeff Farkas <tazberjef@nospam.naxs.net>

### JOB-US-VA-Richmond- COBOL Mainframe Programmer/ Analyst

- **From:** Joe Bedits- Object Systems Group <joe.bedits@osgcorp.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T16:31:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<uvTi6.118867$Tl3.23825523@typhoon.tampabay.rr.com>`

```


Immediate opening available for a COBOL Mainframe Programmer/ Analyst  
in the Richmond, VA area. 

The project will involve analyzing existing mainframe applications and  
preparing it for porting to a Unix platform.

*  Must have experience with COBOL 1 & 2 (77 & 85)
*  Experience with porting is a plus.
*  Experience with Unisys is a plus.

Contract should be 6 mos.-1 year+. Rates are commensurate with  
experience.

Please feel free to send resumes to:

Joe Bedits
Object Systems Group, Inc. (OSG)
joe.bedits@osgcorp.com
(727) 786-3131
F: (727) 773-9683
www.osgcorp.com

Object Systems Group, Incorporated is a technology-based consultancy that
assists Fortune 500 class corporations with the migration and re-engineering
of traditional corporate systems environments to object-oriented based  
systems.
```

  To: Mr. Larry Page, CEO                               2/15/01
      Google.com

Dear Sir:

I wish to strenuously object to the manner of your takeover of
Deja.com.

You either do not comprehend the ways in which Deja.com is used,
or, you have complete and utter contempt for the community which
uses it who can no longer do so in the manner to which they have
been accustomed.

USELESS.

will you kindly restore the Deja version (change the graphics, if 
necessary) so that we may continue to use the forum.   

It is also mildly annoying that you have no provision for sending email
to Google staff other than webmaster and that your business address does
not seem to be on your site, and that you have no toll-free number. Since
it is difficult to communicate with Google, I am posting this here so
that perhaps someone might notice it (unlikely)

R.S.V.P.

Very truly yours,

Anthony Dilworth

<a id="t-bb19c1e836"></a>
## Thread: Unload a DLL - NetExpress 3 x Delphi 5 — 2 msgs

### Unload a DLL - NetExpress 3 x Delphi 5

- **From:** rovani@rech.com.br
- **Date:** 2001-02-15T21:03:51+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96hg7n$s59$1@news.netmar.com>`

```

Hello Friends,

      I'm having problems when trying to unload a DLL. I've created a DLL in
delphi5 with multiple functions (entry points) and "load" it using a
COBOL
procedure pointer as described in the on-line help:
..
77 EXCELDLL PROCEDURE-POINTER.
..
SET EXCELDLL TO ENTRY "RIXLEX.DLL".
..

    I belive that setting EXCELDLL to NULL will unload my dll. Is that
rigth?

    Do I have to use CANCEL in the DLL or in any of it's functions?

    I use Net Express 3.0. Please help me on solve this and thanks in
advance.

PS: Everything in the DLL work's fine, but I want to be sure about unloading
because the DLL execute Excel and it is very "crashable"!!!!

--------------------------------------------
Rovani Marcelo Rech
Rech Inform�tica Ltda / Brazil
rovani@rech.com.br




 -----  Posted via NewsOne.Net: Free (anonymous) Usenet News via the Web  -----
  http://newsone.net/ -- Free reading and anonymous posting to 60,000+ groups
   NewsOne.Net prohibits users from posting spam.  If this or other posts
made through NewsOne.Net violate posting guidelines, email abuse@newsone.net
```

#### ↳ Re: Unload a DLL - NetExpress 3 x Delphi 5

- **From:** "Gael Wilson" <gael.wilson@merant.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-16T09:08:09
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96j00r$kn4$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<96hg7n$s59$1@news.netmar.com>`

```
Rovani,

> ..
> SET EXCELDLL TO ENTRY "RIXLEX.DLL".
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>

No, all that does is set the pointer value to NULL. It does not cause any
additional code to be executed.


>     Do I have to use CANCEL in the DLL or in any of it's functions?

Yes, that is correct. Once all of the loaded programs in the DLL have been
CANCELled the DLL will be unloaded.
```

<a id="t-892c9d5f00"></a>
## Thread: Help a newbie : determine the input type. — 4 msgs

_Topics: [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help a newbie : determine the input type.

- **From:** "MadG" <patrick4133@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-16T22:17:16+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MFhj6.152401$KP3.40041452@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
Newbie Question


How do I determine an input?  I have  a file that has two possibilities for
the record coming in.

Either it is an annual salary and pay adjustment:  PIC 999999V99
                                                                        PIC
9999V99

                  OR

         an hourly .                                               PIC 99V99
                                                                        PIC
99
                                                                        PIC
99
                                                                        PIC
9999V99

How can I test to see if what is coming in is one or the other.
I just learned REDIFINES, so it's all a little awkward to me.

Thanks for any Help!
```

#### ↳ Re: Help a newbie : determine the input type.

- **From:** "Kevin Solomon" <kjsolo@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-17T00:58:13+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<F0kj6.22162$HG.5219273@news4.rdc1.on.home.com>`
- **References:** `<MFhj6.152401$KP3.40041452@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
What is your record file description? For a record of this type you will
probably find you have a single character field, say salary-type for
example, that can only have certain values.

your record >>> data|salary-type|amount|data
your record >>> -----------Y34535-----
or                 >>> -----------H34535-----
and so on...

When you read the record you code an if statement (level 88 entries are
better if you've learned that yet).

IF SALARY-TYPE = "Y"
   MOVE REC-IN-SALARY TO WS-YEARLY-SALARY
ELSE IF SALARY-TYPE = "H"
   MOVE REC-IN-SALARY TO WS-HOURLY-SALARY
END-IF
END-IF.

If you have nothing in the record itself that tells you what type it is
your're really stuck since both fields are numeric with lengths that can
vary.
Hope this helps.
```

#### ↳ Re: Help a newbie : determine the input type.

- **From:** "Kevin Solomon" <kjsolo@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-17T01:05:32+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<w7kj6.22224$HG.5228884@news4.rdc1.on.home.com>`
- **References:** `<MFhj6.152401$KP3.40041452@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
What is your record file description? For a record of this type you will
probably find you have a single character field, say salary-type for
example, that can only have certain values.

your record >>> data|salary-type|amount|data
your record >>> -----------Y34535-----
or                 >>> -----------H34535-----
and so on...

When you read the record you code an if statement (level 88 entries are
better if you've learned that yet).

IF SALARY-TYPE = "Y"
   MOVE REC-IN-SALARY TO WS-YEARLY-SALARY
ELSE IF SALARY-TYPE = "H"
   MOVE REC-IN-SALARY TO WS-HOURLY-SALARY
END-IF
END-IF.

If you have nothing in the record itself that tells you what type it is
your're really stuck since both fields are numeric with lengths that can
vary.
Hope this helps.
```

#### ↳ Re: Help a newbie : determine the input type.

- **From:** Jeff Baynard <jeffbaynard@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-17T01:46:37+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<B6B3425A.716%jeffbaynard@home.com>`
- **References:** `<MFhj6.152401$KP3.40041452@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
Since the decimal point is implied and not strongly-typed, you'll probably
need to determine the record type by testing the value against a range of
values.  Ideally, the file would contain another element on each record
occurence, denoting what type of record it is.

Jeff


in article MFhj6.152401$KP3.40041452@news3.rdc1.on.home.com, MadG at
patrick4133@hotmail.com wrote on 2/16/01 5:17 PM:

> Newbie Question
> 
…[23 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

## Thread: Fujitsu COBOL Compile Problem: F3BH440.DLL (not PATH) — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu COBOL Compile Problem: F3BH440.DLL (not PATH)

- **From:** "Dave" <saltaire@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-17T21:14:12-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96ne9h$dla$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I'm using Fujitsu's free V3 COBOL, and am still trying to compile my COBOL
program.
I was using the Microfocus compiler, ran it through FJ's converter, removed
all the MF compiler directives, fixed all the problems still existing which
the compiler pointed out, and it still won't compile successfully. I'm using
their "Programming Staff" tool, and executing "WINCOB(Compile)" - the only
compiler shown on the menu.

After the compiler thinks for about 8 seconds, it gives me the message:
"F:\...\PCOBOL32\F3BH440.DLL is not found. RC=1157".
The "F..." is the correct path to the compiler and is in the active PATH per
MS/DOS. There are no other messages.

There is no such module anywhere. I can find references in code to "440",
but no references to "F3BH440" or return code "1157" in the manuals or
anywhere else.

Since this free version is supposedly not supported by Fujitsu
(understandably), does anyone have any clues as to what I'm doing wrong? The
program is "plain vanilla" as far as I'm concerned, is less than 6,000 lines
long, uses no features announced in the last 10 or 15 years (except for
STRING which is fairly old, I think).

Any help would be appreciated.

Dave Morton
```

Lars,

If you are using Internet Explorer 4.x and above, try the Tools menu,
Internet Option, Security Tab page,
Go to Scripting,  Active Scripting, and click on the Disable  radio button.
Apply.

I will post in a separate thread for others to know.

Jamal Assaf
USA

When it comes to security Outlook is a sieve.  You need a firewall to block
javascripts from running.  Installing a firewall is even more important if
you have a cable modem with a constant connection like I do.  I use the one
mentioned by Lars-Eric in this thread.  It's freeware and you can get it at
http://www.zonelabs.com/
```

<a id="t-4cd3b8ed70"></a>
## Thread: redefines is giving me hard times newbie help — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax) · [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### redefines is giving me hard times newbie help

- **From:** "MadG" <patrick4133@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-18T17:28:16+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<QCTj6.174637$KP3.44143582@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
and a headache!

 I have in my detail-line a REDEFINES statement that redefines a
group.
 The program ignores the redefined part because I
don't know how to code it.  It looks like this:

01 DETAIL-LINE.
   05 DL-EMP-ID   PIC X(5).
   05 DL-PAY-DETAILS.
       10 DL-HOURLY-RATE  PIC ZZ.99.
   05 DL-PAY-DETAILS-ALT REDEFINES DL-PAY-DETAILS.
       10 DL-DAILY-RATE  PIC ZZ.99.

   My move and print statements:

MOVE DETAIL-LINE TO PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
WRITE PRINT-DETAIL-LINE

   So it ignores the DL-DAILY-RATE all together.  It puts
in whatever was in the buffer from the last read.

Thanks in advance!

AND a SPECIAL Thank-you to Kevin and Jeff.

I was a total klutz!  I have an identifier field, but my head
was up my ...
```

#### ↳ Re: redefines is giving me hard times newbie help

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-18T12:37:35-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96p4pb$thq$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<QCTj6.174637$KP3.44143582@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
Do you understand that REDEFINES means that you have only ONE copy of the
indicated storage?  This means that DL-HOURLY-RATE and DL-DAILY-RATE  point
to the same 5 bytes of storage?  If you understand that, the next question
is HOW to you get data into the two fields?

If your code looks something like,

  Move send-field to DL-DAILY-RATE
  Move other-field to DL-HOURLY-RATE
  MOVE DETAIL-LINE TO PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
  WRITE PRINT-DETAIL-LINE

Then it is always true that you will only see DL-HOURLY-RATE in your
output - because that is the last thing that you put into those 5 byes of
storage.  If on the other hand, your code looks like:

  Move send-field to DL-DAILY-RATE
  MOVE DETAIL-LINE TO PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
  WRITE PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
  Move other-field to DL-HOURLY-RATE
  MOVE DETAIL-LINE TO PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
  WRITE PRINT-DETAIL-LINE

then you should see alternating lines of daily-rate and hourly-rate.

Finally, if you want to see BOTH daily and hourly rates in the same
print-line, then they must not redefine each other.

Does this help?  If not, show us how you "populate" your print-detail-lines
and we might be able to help more/better.
```

```
I am using the student version of Fujitsu 4.0. I have installed the disk
that comes with my text book and have tried a disk my Professor gave me.
Each time I try to execute a program be it a sample or one of our lab
programs I get an error message "missing required F3BlPRCT.DLL". Any ideas
on how I can remedy this situation? Other students seem to be able to make
this software work. Thanks in advance.

Jerry
```

#### ↳ Re: missing execution .dll

- **From:** "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-19T13:53:33+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<xz9k6.2096$HY1.221653@newsread2.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<KuTj6.451$8u5.1525@newsfeed.slurp.net>`

```
There are only two rational possibilities:
1. The required DLL does not exist anywhere on your system, or
2. If it does exist, the program cannot find it.

Can YOU find F3BIPRCT.DLL?

If you cannot find the DLL ("Find" command), then the error is
probably in the installation process. That is, something failed during
the installation of the compiler.

If you can find the DLL by manual searching, then the error probably
lies in the directions you gave the compiler as to the DLL's location
(the PATH statement is a good place to start).

"Jerry" <jjace@fwi.com> wrote in message
news:KuTj6.451$8u5.1525@newsfeed.slurp.net...
> I am using the student version of Fujitsu 4.0. I have installed the
disk
> that comes with my text book and have tried a disk my Professor gave
me.

lab
> programs I get an error message "missing required F3BlPRCT.DLL". Any
ideas
> on how I can remedy this situation? Other students seem to be able
to make
> this software work. Thanks in advance.
>
> Jerry
>
>

PAY
15924  H    13.25              40                 6
12345  S    13.25                                               5.55
56789  H    10.25             40                   3        5.55
38902  S    10.25
35791  H    10.25             40              10
92822  H    15.00              0                            7.74
44474  H    10.00              0                            7.74


     NUMBER OF SALARIED EMPLOYEES  :     2
        NUMBER OF HOURLY EMPLOYEES   :    5
                     TOTAL NET PAY  :          $.00

#### ↳ Re: Redefines hard times,, more info

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-18T18:01:41-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96pnos$s4e$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<ihYj6.177998$KP3.44704384@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
Gotcha <G>

If you look at your logic when
   PF-PAY-CODE = 1
you will see that you never move anything to
 DL-HOURLY-RATE
not to its redefinition in
 DL-ANNUAL-SALARY

Therefore, it will "keep" whatever was that storage location from the last
print line.

There are several ways that you could "solve" this.

A) Move spaces to your entire Working-Storage area after each write (so it
is cleared out before the next one)

B) Make certain that you move SOMETHING to every byte in your WS area before
doing a print from that record.

C) (probably what you want to do) "fix" the following lines in your logic:

    MOVE PF-ANNUAL-SALARY TO
          DL-PAY-ADJUST-S
    MOVE PF-PAY-ADJUST-S TO
        DL-PAY-ADJUST-S

I'll *bet* that you meant to have that first move go to
  DL-ANNUAL-SALARY
      and not to
 DL-PAY-ADJUST-S

P.S.  This is a "common" error even among "experienced" COBOL programmers.
You copy logic from one place to another and "forget" to make all the
required changes.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Thank-YOU!

- **From:** "MadG" <patrick4133@hotmail.com>

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<ihYj6.177998$KP3.44704384@news3.rdc1.on.home.com> <96pnos$s4e$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```

I can't believe how easy it is to over look something soooo simple.

And Jill,   Why yes I do!

client socket on a workstation.  Of course we need to translate the data from
our mainframe (ebcdic) to the client(ascii) and vise-versa.  
We are using calls to EZACIC04/EZACIC05 with TS 1.3 and it works fine.

pages for ascci and ebcdic : my application manages some labels with french
words with accentuation (�,�...) :they are being transformed into some ugly
'{' or '}' after the conversion from ebcdic into ascii. It seems there's some
default code pages (ebcdic 500 I think) taken from somewhere ?
Is it possible to set their value ?

#### ↳ Re: translate COBOL to C

- **From:** "Ira D. Baxter" <idbaxter@semdesigns.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-20T07:32:30-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<t94s6g7cddbk0f@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<3A9144BF.AD07F656@alcatel.fr>`

```
The DMS Reengineering Toolkit is generalized compiler technology used
to automatate analysis, modification and translation of large-scale software
sources.
DMS provides parsing, tree-generation, tree-walking, attribute-evaluation,
source-language-level transformations, prettyprinting, and a lot more.
DMS has off the shelf parsers/prettyprinters for COBOL, C, C++, Java, XML,
SQL and many other legacy languages.

See http://www.semdesigns.com/Products/DMS/DMSToolkit.html.

It could be used to effect a reliable translation.  Usually, such
translations
are custom, because customers want to translate applications using
execution environment assumptions, so the problem isn't just translating
the source code; one also wants to translates the "assumptions" (i.e,
replace system calls for one kind of system with possibly different
calls for the new target system.

This also only makes sense if the translation scale is some tens of
thousands
of lines or more; otherwise, a hand translation is about as cost effective.
```

## Thread: quick help question — 11 msgs

### quick help question

- **From:** deek40@aol.com (Deek40)
- **Date:** 2001-02-19T16:07:35+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010219110735.02143.00000021@ng-cm1.aol.com>`

```
Hello once again, I am a student and trying to learn cobol, when i compile my
program i got like 50 errors ok np i fixed all the ones i could but there are a
few i cant get rid of.  Please help because i have put so much time into this
and it would kill me to lose 2 out of the 10 points for these minor errors. 
Thanks for your time.

here are the errors:
1)user word 'Detail  Desc' is multidefined
here is the code it points to:
PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
		UNTIL A-SUB > 3
	#######	MOVE  DETAIL-DESC (A-SUB)  TO DESC-PR########
		MOVE  PROD-UNITS (A-SUB)   TO UNITS-PR
		MOVE  PROD-REVENUE (A-SUB) TO REVENUE-PR
		WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM DETAIL-LINE AFTER 1
     END-PERFORM.
2) user word 'summary-line' is multi defined
here is the code it points to:
 PERFORM VARYING S-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
          UNTIL S-SUB > 6
         MOVE PRODUCT-LINE (S-SUB) TO SUM-PROD-LINE         
           PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
             UNTIL A-SUB > 3
                MOVE LINE-REVENUE (S-SUB  A-SUB) TO SUM-REV-PR (S-SUB)   
           END-PERFORM
##### WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM SUMMARY-LINE AFTER 1####### 
      END-PERFORM.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
--------------------
here is my full code please help me because i have put countless hours into
this program and i need the 2 points that i get for no errors in compilation.  

 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
 PROGRAM-ID. projone.
***********************************
*PURPOSE: SOFTWARE COST ANALYSIS  *
*AUTHOR: DERRICK PIZUR            *
*WRITTEN: 02/02/01.               *
*REVISED:.                        *
*COMPILER: FUJITSU COBOL 4.0      *
*NOTES:.                          *
***********************************
 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
 CONFIGURATION SECTION.
 SOURCE-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
 OBJECT-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
 
 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.
	   SELECT SOFTWARE-FILE
		ASSIGN TO "A:\PROJ11-6\SOFT.DAT"
		ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
	   SELECT PRINT-FILE
		ASSIGN TO "A:\PROJ11-6\REPORT.TXT"
		ORGANIZATION LINE SEQUENTIAL.
 DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
 FD SOFTWARE-FILE
	   RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS.
 01 SOFTWARE-RECORD.
    05 SOFT-PROGRAM-INFO.
	    10 SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE         PIC X.
      10 SOFT-PRODUCT-NO           PIC 9(4).
      10 SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME         PIC X(18).
    05 SOFT-VARIABLE-COSTS.
	    10 SOFT-PREP-COSTS.
		15 SOFT-LOADING-PER-DISK PIC 9V99.
    	15 SOFT-NO-DISKS-USED    PIC 99.
      10 SOFT-MANUAL-PRINTING      PIC 99V99.
      10 SOFT-SHIPPING-N-HANDLING  PIC 99V99.
    05 SOFT-SELL-PRICE		 PIC 999V99.
    05 SOFT-FIXED-COST       	 PIC 9(5).
 FD PRINT-FILE.
 01 PRINT-RECORD PIC X(80).
 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
 01 subscripts.
     05 A-SUB   PIC 99  VALUE ZERO.
     05 S-SUB PIC 99  VALUE ZERO.
 01 TITLE-LINE-1.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(15) VALUE SPACES.
	   05 FILLER      PIC X(5)  VALUE "Nexus".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(1)  VALUE SPACE.
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(8)  VALUE "Software".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(1)  VALUE SPACE.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(4)  VALUE "INC.".
 01 TITLE-LINE-2.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(15) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(13) VALUE "Product Cost ".
     05 FILLER      PIC X(9)  VALUE "Analysis ".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(6)  VALUE "as of "  .
     05 FILLER	  PIC 9(2)  VALUE 02.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(1)  VALUE "/".
     05 FILLER      PIC 9(2)  VALUE 14.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(1)  VALUE "/".
     05 FILLER      PIC 9(2)  VALUE 01.
 01 TITLE-LINE-3.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(9)  VALUE "based on ".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(6)  VALUE "Total ".
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(6)  VALUE "Fixed ".
     05 FILLER	  PIC x(6)  VALUE "Costs:".
     05 TOT-FIXED-COSTS PIC $ZZ,ZZ9.
 01 TITLE-LINE-SUM-2.
     05 FILLER      PIC X(21) VALUE "Product Cost Analysis".
 01 TITLE-LINE-SUM-3.
     05 FILLER	  PIC X(36) VALUE "Summary Report by Product Line as of".
     
 01 TITLE-LINE-SUM-4.
     05 FILLER     PIC X(7) VALUE "Product".
     05 FILLER     PIC X(2) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER     PIC X(9) VALUE "Breakeven".
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(4) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER     PIC X(7) VALUE "Profit:".
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(6) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(5) VALUE "Price".
 01 TITLE-LINE-SUM-5.
     05 FILLER     PIC X(2) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(4) VALUE "Line".
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(26) VALUE SPACES.
     05 FILLER	 PIC X(8) VALUE "Decline:".
        
 01 ANALYSIS-TABLE.
     05 PRODUCT-INFORMATION OCCURS 3 TIMES.
        10 PROD-UNITS   PIC 9(5).
        10 PROD-REVENUE PIC 9(8).
 01 SUMMARY-TABLE.
      05 SUMMARY-LINE OCCURS 6 TIMES.
        10  PRODUCT-LINE          PIC X .
        10  PRODUCT-INFO                OCCURS 3 TIMES. 
        	    15  LINE-REVENUE          PIC 9(5).

 01 DETAIL-TABLE.
      05  FILLER          PIC X(20) VALUE 'Breakeven'.
	    05  FILLER          PIC X(20) VALUE 'Profit $50,000'.
      05  FILLER          PIC X(20) VALUE 'Price Decline 25%'.
* I READ AHREAD IN THE TEXT TO THE NEXT CHAPTER AND USED
* TEACH YOURSELF COBOL IN 21 DAYS AS REFERENCE FOR THE REDEFINES 
* USEAGE.
 01  FILLER REDEFINES DETAIL-TABLE.
	    05  DETAIL-DESC PIC X(20) OCCURS 3 TIMES.
 
 01 WS-ACCUMULATORS.
     05 WS-VARIABLE-COSTS     PIC 9(5) VALUE ZERO.
     05 WS-PROFIT-PER-SALE    PIC 9(8) VALUE ZERO.
     05 WS-REVENUE	    PIC 9(8) VALUE ZERO.
	   05 WS-UNITS     	    PIC 9(5) VALUE ZERO.
     05  WS-SUM-TOTALS OCCURS 3 TIMES.
          10  WS-TOTAL             PIC 9(6).
     05 WS-DECLINE	    PIC 9(5) VALUE ZERO.
 01 DETAIL-LINE.
    05  DETAIL-DESC           PIC X(20).
    05  UNITS-PR              PIC ZZZ9.
    05  REVENUE-PR            PIC ZZ,ZZZ,ZZ9.
* CHANGE PICS LENGTH
 01  SUMMARY-LINE.
      05  SUM-PROD-LINE          PIC X.
      05  SUM-REVENUE OCCURS 3 TIMES.
            10  FILLER                   PIC XXX.
             10  SUM-REV-PR        PIC $$$$,$$9.
 01 DETAIL-LINES.
   05 PRODUCT-DL. 
      10 FILLER	              PIC X(13) VALUE "Product Line:".
      10 FILLER 	              PIC X(1)  VALUE SPACE.
      10 DL-SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE   PIC X     VALUE SPACE.
   05 PROGRAM-NAME-DL.
      10 FILLER		      PIC X(14) VALUE "Product Name: ". 
      10 DL-SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME   PIC X(18) VALUE SPACES.
   05 SELL-PRICE-DL.
      10 FILLER     	      PIC X(12) VALUE "Sell Price: ".
      10 DL-SOFT-SELL-PRICE     PIC $$$,$$9.99.
      10 FILLER                 PIC X(21) VALUE "Total Variable Cost: ". 
      10 DL-SOFT-VARIABLE-COST  PIC ZZ9.99.
 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
 MAINLINE.
 	   PERFORM 100-INITIALIZATION.
     PERFORM 200-MAIN-PROCESS 
     PERFORM 600-PRINT-DETAIL-SUM.
     CLOSE SOFTWARE-FILE.
     CLOSE PRINT-FILE.   	
	   	 STOP RUN.
 100-INITIALIZATION.
* INSERT DATE LOGIC
     OPEN INPUT SOFTWARE-FILE
          OUTPUT PRINT-FILE.

     
     PERFORM VARYING S-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
          UNTIL S-SUB > 6
         MOVE SPACES TO PRODUCT-LINE (S-SUB)
         PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
            UNTIL A-SUB > 3
                 MOVE 0 TO WS-TOTAL (A-SUB)        
         END-PERFORM
     END-PERFORM.



	   PERFORM 400-CLEAR-ANALYSIS.
     READ SOFTWARE-FILE. 
     PERFORM 300-LINE-BREAK.
 200-MAIN-PROCESS.
      
      IF SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE NOT = PRODUCT-LINE (S-SUB)
			PERFORM 300-LINE-BREAK
	   	END-IF.

	    MOVE 0 TO A-SUB.

      PERFORM 201-VARIABLE-COST-CALC.
      PERFORM 202-BREAKEVEN-CALC.
 	    PERFORM 203-REVENUE-CALC.
      PERFORM 204-MOVE-TO-TABLES.

      PERFORM 205-PROFIT-UNITS.
      PERFORM 203-REVENUE-CALC.
      PERFORM 204-MOVE-TO-TABLES.

      PERFORM 206-DECLINE-CALC.
      PERFORM 203-REVENUE-CALC.
      PERFORM 204-MOVE-TO-TABLES.

      PERFORM 500-PRINT-DETAIL.
      

 201-VARIABLE-COST-CALC.
     COMPUTE WS-VARIABLE-COSTS = ((SOFT-LOADING-PER-DISK + 1) * 
             SOFT-NO-DISKS-USED) + SOFT-MANUAL-PRINTING +
		   SOFT-SHIPPING-N-HANDLING
     END-COMPUTE.

 202-BREAKEVEN-CALC.
     COMPUTE WS-UNITS = (SOFT-SELL-PRICE - 
    	   WS-VARIABLE-COSTS) / SOFT-FIXED-COST
     END-COMPUTE.

 203-REVENUE-CALC.
     COMPUTE WS-REVENUE = (WS-UNITS * 
             SOFT-SELL-PRICE)
     END-COMPUTE.
     
 204-MOVE-TO-TABLES.
     ADD 1 TO A-SUB.
     MOVE WS-UNITS TO PROD-UNITS (A-SUB).
     MOVE WS-REVENUE TO PROD-REVENUE (A-SUB).
	   ADD WS-REVENUE TO LINE-REVENUE (S-SUB A-SUB).
     ADD WS-REVENUE TO WS-TOTAL (A-SUB).
 205-PROFIT-UNITS.
     COMPUTE WS-UNITS = (50000 + SOFT-FIXED-COST) /
	     (SOFT-SELL-PRICE - WS-VARIABLE-COSTS)
	   END-COMPUTE.

 206-DECLINE-CALC.
     COMPUTE WS-DECLINE = (SOFT-SELL-PRICE * .75)
	   END-COMPUTE.

 300-LINE-BREAK.
     ADD 1 TO S-SUB.
*     MOVE SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE TO (S-SUB).

 400-CLEAR-ANALYSIS.
	   PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
     	UNTIL A-SUB > 3
	 	   MOVE 0 TO PROD-UNITS (A-SUB)
		   MOVE 0 TO PROD-REVENUE (A-SUB)
     END-PERFORM.

 500-PRINT-DETAIL.
     MOVE SOFT-FIXED-COST  TO TOT-FIXED-COSTS.
     MOVE SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE TO DL-SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE.
     MOVE SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME TO DL-SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME.
     MOVE SOFT-SELL-PRICE   TO DL-SOFT-SELL-PRICE.
     MOVE WS-VARIABLE-COSTS TO DL-SOFT-VARIABLE-COST.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-1 AFTER PAGE.
	   WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-2 AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-3 AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TOT-FIXED-COSTS AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM DL-SOFT-PRODUCT-LINE AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM DL-SOFT-PROGRAM-NAME AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM DL-SOFT-SELL-PRICE  AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM DL-SOFT-VARIABLE-COST AFTER 1.
     PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
		UNTIL A-SUB > 3
		MOVE  DETAIL-DESC (A-SUB)  TO DESC-PR
		MOVE  PROD-UNITS (A-SUB)   TO UNITS-PR
		MOVE  PROD-REVENUE (A-SUB) TO REVENUE-PR
		WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM DETAIL-LINE AFTER 1
     END-PERFORM.
     PERFORM 400-CLEAR-ANALYSIS.
 600-PRINT-DETAIL-SUM.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-1     AFTER PAGE.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-SUM-2 AFTER 1. 
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-SUM-3 AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-SUM-4 AFTER 1.
     WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM TITLE-LINE-SUM-5 AFTER 1.
      PERFORM VARYING S-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
          UNTIL S-SUB > 6
         MOVE PRODUCT-LINE (S-SUB) TO SUM-PROD-LINE         
           PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
             UNTIL A-SUB > 3
                MOVE LINE-REVENUE (S-SUB  A-SUB) TO SUM-REV-PR (S-SUB)   
           END-PERFORM
       WRITE PRINT-RECORD FROM SUMMARY-LINE AFTER 1 
      END-PERFORM.

     MOVE 'TOTAL' TO SUM-PROD-LINE   
     PERFORM VARYING A-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
      UNTIL A-SUB > 3
         MOVE WS-TOTAL (A-SUB) TO SUM-REV-PR (A-SUB)
     END-PERFORM.
```

- **From:** Jeff Farkas <tazberjef@naxs.nospam.net>

#### ↳ Re: quick help question

- **From:** Howard Brazee <howard@brazee.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-19T10:57:43-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A915E97.D351FBE0@brazee.net>`
- **References:** `<20010219110735.02143.00000021@ng-cm1.aol.com>`

```


Deek40 wrote:

> Hello once again, I am a student and trying to learn cobol, when i compile my
> program i got like 50 errors ok np i fixed all the ones i could but there are a
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>      END-PERFORM.

But that's not where it is defined.  Even though one of your DETAIL-DESC is within
an occurs, and the other was not, CoBOL won't make any assumptions on what you
want.  It needs an unambiguous definition.  If you have more than one DETAIL-DESC
(a practice which is frowned upon in many shops), you need to qualify your
statement with an OF or IN.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: quick help question

- **From:** deek40@aol.com (Deek40)
- **Date:** 2001-02-19T18:39:45+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010219133945.04298.00000507@ng-ck1.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<3A915E97.D351FBE0@brazee.net>`

```

for the help.

#### ↳ Re: quick help question

- **From:** "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-19T22:04:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<GLgk6.6$rX4.980@newsread1.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<20010219110735.02143.00000021@ng-cm1.aol.com>`

```

"Deek40" <deek40@aol.com>

> 1)user word 'Detail  Desc' is multidefined

Silly compiler!

It doesn't point to the multiple definitions (in the Data Division)
but tries to confuse you by pointing to where these multiple
definitions are used (the Procedure Division).

But, then again, just as a falling tree makes no sound if there's no
one to hear (according to some), there is no error with multiple
definitions unless you try to use one.

Anyway, look in the data-forest, not the procedure-forest.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: quick help question

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-02-19T17:33:53-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96sah0$oud$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<20010219110735.02143.00000021@ng-cm1.aol.com> <GLgk6.6$rX4.980@newsread1.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`

```
There is nothing "illegal" with having multiple definitions of the same
items in the data division - so it can't issue a message there.  These are
legal for two reasons:

1) (most common) you use "qualification" ("OF/IN" phrase) when referencing a
data item in the procedure division.  Very COMMON if you define your record
layouts in "copy books".

2) (less common - but has its reason)  If you do NOT reference a data item
in the procedure division, it is "totally legal" (but not usually useful) to
have as many multiple definitions - even at the 01-level - or unable to be
qualified - in the data division.  (FYI - this was a change between the '74
and '85 Standards, so those of you still using '74 compilers may not have
this "feature" <G>)
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: quick help question

- **From:** Howard Brazee <howard@brazee.net>
- **Date:** 2001-02-20T08:11:35-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3A928927.B26C69D0@brazee.net>`
- **References:** `<20010219110735.02143.00000021@ng-cm1.aol.com> <GLgk6.6$rX4.980@newsread1.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`

```
Jerry P wrote:

> "Deek40" <deek40@aol.com>
>
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> definitions unless you try to use one.

Unless you try to use one incorrectly (ambiguously).  You can still use
them with qualifiers.

> Anyway, look in the data-forest, not the procedure-forest.
```

## Thread: Database Connection — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### Database Connection

- **From:** cobolchappy@yahoo.com (Cobol Boy)
- **Date:** 2001-02-19T21:46:15+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010219204613.3625.qmail@web12712.mail.yahoo.com>`

```
Hello All,

I am using the free Fujitsu compiler and am wanting my
program to retrieve some data from a database that I
use for my Delphi work. The database is a Paradox
database and I have the ODBC driver.

What I need to know is how do I set up my Cobol
envioronment so that the program knows where to find
my database and talk to it.

I've checked the online manuals on the CD but I seem
to be going round in circles, which is why I've posted
the question here in case someone has done this sort
of thing before and can give me a quick and simple
step by step guide to how to set this up.

Thanks very much for your time.

John.

__________________________________________________
Do You Yahoo!?
Get personalized email addresses from Yahoo! Mail - only $35 
a year!  http://personal.mail.yahoo.com/
```

- **Message-ID:** `<3A91A03C.1ED3E90C@boeing.com>`
- **References:** `<20010219204613.3625.qmail@web12712.mail.yahoo.com>`

```

```

#### ↳ Re: Database Connection

- **From:** floydj@netins.net (Floyd Johnson)
- **Date:** 2001-02-19T23:46:08+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<96sb80$o9v$1@ins21.netins.net>`
- **References:** `<20010219204613.3625.qmail@web12712.mail.yahoo.com>`

```
There are three steps:

1.  Identify the ODBC data source - look for "ODBC Data Sources" under
Control Panel in Win 95.  I think it is one more layer down in Win
2000.

2.  Define the data source to the COBOL program using sqlodbc.exe, located
in the fujitsu directory structure.

3.  Issue the appropriate COBOL and SQL commands to access the database.

I am not sure how I figured this all out, but much of it came from the
fujitsu manual - other hints came from other ODBC tutorials. You might
want to look at material on using JDBC for other hints.

Floyd Johnson
